title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources My Benefits Information about the range of benefits and entitlements available to current members of University staff. ‌‌‌‌‌‌‌ ‌‌‌‌‌‌‌‌‌ ‌‌ ‌‌‌‌‌‌ Health and Wellbeing Staff Discounts Pensions ‌ ‌‌‌‌‌‌ ‌ ‌‌‌‌‌ ‌‌‌‌‌‌‌ Leave Travel Family Friendly Health and Wellbeing Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) Speak in confidence to an independent and fully qualified counsellor 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year to discuss any emotional, personal or work-related problems. Talking to one of Vivup’s experienced counsellors or psychotherapists can help you understand a problem, make a tough decision, try a different approach or just learn how to cope with a difficult situation. The aim is to support you and find a solution to whatever is worrying you. Ravelin Sports Centre The University has an outstanding new sports centre. Students, staff and local residents are all able to enjoy the facilities, with a range of classes and courses, personal training and very affordable membership with great benefits available. Staff members can access discounted membership. Dentistry The Dental Academy provides dental care by students to patients who do not currently have a dentist and who are available for multiple, potentially long appointments during the day. You can check on the NHS Dental Helpline website to find out if the Academy is currently taking on new patients. Bupa University Scheme New Bupa University Scheme, offered by Uni-Health and available to all University staff and their families. Some of the benefits include: Comprehensive Cover including Full In-patient & Out-patient Cover / Full Cancer Cover / Full UK

title: Policies
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: Access to Information > Policies Policies The Document Warehouse What's in the Document Warehouse? The Document Warehouse is intended to house the University's university-wide documents: strategies, policies, procedures and codes which affect all staff in their work. See the A-Z list. What's not in the Document Warehouse? Some items only appear on the external facing policy page found here; https://www.port.ac.uk/about-us/structure-and-governance/policies-and-standards The Document Warehouse does not include operational documentation for every single process or department. More information can be found on the relevant University academic, support or service department web page:https://staff.port.ac.uk/staffessentials/departments/ What if I'm not sure what I'm looking for or where to find it? If you can't find what you are looking for, or if you are not sure that you have the latest version then please contact Jacqui Bryden at jacqui.bryden@port.ac.uk. title: Printing Services
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Printing Services Printing Services The Design, Print and Publications Team provides the complete printing services for staff and students. Printing covers anything we require created or branded, from all paper based products (business cards, leaflets, posters, prospectuses) through to merchandise, exhibition materials, window graphics and building signage. We no longer print on campus but have a range of external suppliers to ensure that work is priced competitively and efficiently managed across all our printing needs. To get started, please fill in a Hornbill request. Staff Office printing For printing that you would have undertaken on your office printer (e.g. over 50 pages) simply attach a PDF of your work to the Hornbill form, with your cost centre, and delivery address and we will deliver your print requirements to campus (or home). Academic printing We support academic colleagues with production of teaching and learning materials as well as submitted coursework printing. Please raise a Hornbill request. Printing for Students We provide a cost-effective and quality printing service for our students. You can also print your posters, and bind your theses (or dissertations) to University specifications through our University Online Store. Contact us: Email: designandprint@port.ac.uk Explore UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Customer Relationship Management
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: Departments > Service Departments Customer Relationship Management Customer Relationship Management (CRM) One view of our University. One view of our customers. Better outcomes for everyone. CRM is an approach that allows us to manage and analyse our interactions with past, current, and potential customers (including students, alumni, businesses and any other individuals or organisations the University comes into contact with). There is 1 CRM software solution we use to do this, called Salesforce. There are also 5 other software solutions that support customer relationship management and work alongside Salesforce. These are: SITS – Current applicants and students (data and communications) Pure – Research and Innovation (data and engagement) Target Connect – Careers and Employability platform for jobs, placements, volunteering, events and appointments Eventbrite – Events (excluding Student Recruitment events) Campaign Monitor – Income generating R&I bulk communications, and other communications to mailing lists of over 500 where there's a really good reason that the other systems listed above can't be used! All six software solutions will help us maximise and streamline our activities to support our relationships with all our customers, achieve our data compliance targets, reduce data entry, and develop strategic partnerships and collaborate more closely with organisations. This will support us in implementing the University Strategy. For more information and guidance on Student communications for colleagues, including the channel options and responsible teams/departments for student communications, please visit the Student Communications Guidance webpage. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Occupational Health Service Human Resources Occupational Health Service What is Occupational Health? 'The promotion and maintenance of the highest degree of physical, mental and social wellbeing of workers in all occupations by preventing departures from health, controlling risks and the adaptation of work to people and people to their jobs.' Professor Dame Carol Black, FRCP - Adviser to the British Government on Work and Health (2006-2016) The University’s Occupational Health Service provides advice to managers and individuals on work related health problems and health problems that can affect work further to referral from a Manager. Occupational Health is a distinct branch of health care which specialises in the relationship between work and health. Advice to management will only include medical information with the consent of the employee but will mainly focus on advice regarding work fitness and any adjustments that might support the employee. All information kept by the University's Occupational Health Service is held in line with the University's Data Retention Policy. For further information, please read the Occupational Health Privacy Statement (pdf). Contact us: To contact the Occupational Health Service, please call on 011 8207 6190 or email: admin@cordellhealth.co.uk Explore HR HomepageMy RoleLeaving My RoleManaging My TeamHR Business Partnering TeamLearning and DevelopmentHR SystemsMy Rewards and BenefitsPensionsPayrollOccupational Health Service Role of Occupational Health Referral Sickness absence Risk Assessment for New and Expectant Mothers Mental Health Bullying & Harassment Information Job Hazard Information Protocols

title: Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP)
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: Special > Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP) Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP) AHEP at the University of Portsmouth The Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP) aims to promote excellence in HE management and administration and to raise the profile of those roles in HE as a profession through the development of best practice and high standards in the area. Not only are they a representative voice for HE Professionals in the sector, they also provide support and guidance for members in developing your their careers. Find out more here. The AHEP core commitment for members are to: Empower your career journey Improving your professional practice Supporting your professional development Championing the vaules of: Credible, Collaborative, Inclusive and Personable They provide a Professional Framework linked to 8 key professional behaviours, which can be used whatever your role or level. Membership is open to everyone, whether you are and existing or aspiring HE Professional. Click here to find out more about why you should join, or here to explore the membership levels available. To find out more check out the National Events and local activities via our Padlet AUA@UoP Community. If you have any questions or would like to find out more, please contact the UoP Branch Advocate: Nikki Sowerby. Nikki is also the South Region Network Deputy Coordinator. body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Staff Essentials Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. Home Staff Services Departments Resources The University provides a number of staff networks intended to support issues relating to harassment, bullying and intimidation in the workplace, or more general counselling and guidance, available at any time of the day or night. A 24- hour confidential telephone helpline is available to all University staff through the University Employee Assistance Programme. You can access information on health, wellbeing and support from MyPort The University Chaplains are trained to listen and be alongside people at the high and low points of life in a non-judgemental way. They work confidentially across the University with staff and students. As part of the University's commitment to equal opportunities, the Equality and Diversity Unit has supported the development of a number of staff networks. These exist to allow staff from similar backgrounds to meet, network and discuss the issues that matter to them. © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Staff Networks Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Staff Networks Staff networks are pivotal in building an inclusive culture at the University, where everyone is able to thrive. The networks are supported by the Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion team. Staff networks allow colleagues space to come together to exchange views, ideas, resources, support and advice, as well as providing a platform to raise awareness and instigate positive change within the University. Networks are run for staff by staff and are inclusive spaces open to all colleagues, no assumptions are made about individuals because they join a community. Representatives of the staff networks sit on the University's EDI consultative group, EDI steering committee, University Athena SWAN and REC SAT groups, where the networks are invited to present information on their work and views to these committees. To join a network click the relevant Join Network button and complete the membership form, this goes straight to the Co-Chairs who can provide you access to the network Spaces. Home Staff Services Departments Resources ‌‌As a University employee you are automatically a member of either the Academic Staff Association (ASA) or the Professional Support Staff Association (PSSA). Both associations promote a spirit of unity and communication, represent the views and champion the wellbeing of all staff, and facilitate two-way communication between staff and the University's management structure, including the Board of Governors and Directorate. What can we assist you with? Advising you on the best person/department to speak in regards to any issues and concerns you may have. Highlighting issues that are of general concern to senior management. Finding out more about the departments and services provided across the university. For more information, please visit the ASA or PSSA websites. © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Staff Essentials
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Staff Essentials Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. Home Staff Services Departments Resources Support for colleagues and students during conflict, humanitarian, and natural disasters We are proud to be a university with a strong global outlook and deep local roots. From our earliest days, our community has included people from every corner of the globe. This global connection means when conflicts, humanitarian crises, or natural disasters occur anywhere in the world the impact is felt by our students, staff and alumni here in Portsmouth and beyond. World events can have a profound impact on our wellbeing, peace of mind and our sense of connection to others. We understand that everyone’s experience is unique, and that responses to such events may change over time. During these times of uncertainty, it's important to remember that seeking support is a positive step and a sign of strength. We encourage you to stay connected with friends, family, and colleagues, and please ask for help if you need it. Whether you are concerned about family and friends in affected regions, processing difficult news and emotions or finding it challenging to concentrate on your studies or work we encourage you to reach out. Help and advice for colleagues affected by home and world events This guide is designed to help those affected by world events, whether seeking support for themselves or looking for ways to support others. Our university stands united in supporting one another through these difficult times. As a community, we draw strength from our diversity and shared commitment to understanding and supporting one another. We understand that each p

title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Equity, Diversity & Inclusion With students arriving at the University of Portsmouth from over 120 countries each year and the University's commitment to widening participation in higher education attracting an ever-increasing variety of students - and indeed colleagues - understanding and accommodating their needs is essential to its continued success. The Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion unit undertakes a wide variety of work to achieve this. We offer: Advice, support and training for colleagues, to ensure the University meets its legislative requirements and its commitment to create conditions where all colleagues and students are treated only based on their merits, abilities and potential. Access to policies, guidelines and the University Equality Objectives, which outline how we work to meet our equity, diversity, and inclusion requirements. Guidance during the development and review of University policies, procedures and functions to ensure they don't disadvantage any group of colleagues, students, prospective colleagues, prospective students or graduates, and where possible, improve equality of opportunity. This review is titled Equality Impact Assessment (EIA). Staff Networks which enable staff from similar backgrounds to meet and discuss the issues affecting them, as well as offering a platform to initiate positive change in the University. Coordination and training of a Harassment Adviser Network to offer help and advice to students and staff who believe they may be a victim of bullying or harassment. Liaison with students, staff, other HE institutions and the wider city to help organise events and activities which c

A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. Sustainable travel Leaving the car at home and making sustainable travel choices can save you time and money. You may also benefit from improved wellbeing and reduce your environmental impact at the same time. Whether you’re looking for information about local cycle routes to work, walking maps or links to public transport you can access it and plan your journey on the My Journey Hampshire website. You can also commute together with others by using easitShare. This scheme covers journeys made by car, bicycle, taxi, motorbikes, train, buses and on foot. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ Explore HR HomepageMy RoleLeaving My RoleManaging My TeamHR Business Partnering TeamLearning and DevelopmentHR SystemsMy Rewards and BenefitsPensionsPayrollOccupational Health ServiceEquality and DiversityContact usStaff Surveys HR A-Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Equality and Diversity training Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Equality and Diversity training The Equality and Diversity Unit provide a wide range of training/briefings, including staff induction, e-learning, management briefings and bespoke sessions as requested by business areas. All Staff Core Training 1. All staff, as part of their core training requirements, should undertake the following equality e-learning within their probationary period: Bullying & Harassment (and an additional session for managers) Diversity & Inclusion in the Workplace Equality, Diversity & Inclusion in the Workplace (for managers) Race in the Workplace Safeguarding in Higher Education Sexual Harassment Awareness Access Core and Role-specific training from Docebo. Additional training, including Equality Impact Assessment (EIA), Menopause, Disability, Dementia, and Autism Awareness, can be accessed via Docebo. 2. Staff may also consider completing the Harvard Bias Implicit Analysis Test. This anonymous test is for your information only and is not a requirement of University training. 3. The Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion Team will introduce all staff to the support offered during the staff induction event that all new staff attend. Bespoke Sessions The Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion team can offer bespoke tailored sessions for team meeting updates or specific sessions. To discuss this further, please email the Equality Manager at equality@port.ac.uk If you have any problems with the e-learning procedure, please contact equality@port.ac.uk. title: Academic Registry
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Academic Registry Academic Registry About Us and Who to Contact Welcome to the Academic Registry! The Academic Registry plays a central role in our institution’s commitment to maintaining academic excellence. Our role is to develop and support an academic quality regulatory landscape which supports the highest standards of academic quality, through academic regulations and policies that support a fair, transparent, and effective learning environment. Our Mission Our Mission is to ensure the University’s academic quality environment complies with mandatory regulatory requirements, upholds academic standards, enhances the student experience, and supports all students, regardless of their background, to succeed. Our Values Academic Excellence: Promoting the highest standards of education and quality of student experience Integrity: Ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability in all our actions. Collaboration: Partnering with students, staff and external stakeholders to uphold and improve academic quality. Responsiveness: Adapting to the changing landscape of higher education through innovation and foresight. Our Objectives Working collaboratively across the University, and informed by sector leading-practice we will: Ensure all academic policies, regulations and procedures meet mandatory regulatory requirements whilst being efficient and maintaining a forward-thinking approach. Provide an assessment regulatory framework which supports student achievement, and eliminates unwarranted barriers to learning and success; Create a student and staff-centric academic quality environment that promotes high standards and continuous improvement; Generate and a

title: Estates and Campus Services
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Estates and Campus Services Estates and Campus Services We are responsible for the construction and maintenance of the University's estate. The estate is, broadly speaking, based on two main campuses, these being Guildhall and Langstone Student Village and extends to approximately 1.5 million sq ft of buildings together with associated playing fields and open parkland. The building stock extends to some 70 main buildings together with sundry additional minor structures. These range from Victorian buildings, either purpose built for education use or converted from original uses, to modern purpose built academic and residential buildings. The department recently completed a large development programme together with the resultant major refurbishment of existing building stock and subsequent departmental moves. It is also currently responsible for the implementation of the University's Mobility Policy. As a department providing a service to the University we recognise the importance of making you aware of the following: Who we are What services we provide How to obtain them What to do if you have any problems To help achieve this we have put together this web site that we hope will be of use to you. Please use the links to find the information you are looking for. The site will be updated as appropriate. Please let your colleagues know that this facility is available to them. We hope that you find these pages of use and would appreciate any feedback that you may wish to give. Please send any comments to Chris Burke-Hynes. title: Global
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Global Global UoP Global is responsible for the University's support of international students, overseas partnerships and recruitment of new students from outside of the UK. It consists of several teams, dealing with wide-ranging international aspects from admissions and recruitment to visa support, student engagement, marketing and international exchange and study abroad partnerships. With a recently revamped structure, we work to deliver the aims of the Global Engagement Strategy through our activities here in the UK, as well as at our international offices across the globe. If you would like to discuss matters relating to Global activity you can contact Bobby Mehta, Director of Uop Global (bobby.mehta@port.ac.uk) or Mark Ovens, Deputy Director of UoP Global (mark.ovens@port.ac.uk). To speak with a specific team please see the information below. Admin Team The Admin Team deliver outstanding customer service to enquiring students and colleagues around the University (as well as external agents and partner organisations) and providing comprehensive administrative support to the wider department, ensuring all policies and procedures are carried out in accordance with University rules and regulations and aligning with best practice. Learn more Exchanges and Study Abroad The Exchanges and Study abroad team provides advice and support to all students interested in studying or working abroad as part of their degree, as well as providing opportunities for Academic and Professional staff to undertake teaching or training activities with the Erasmus+ programme. The team is also responsible and the first point of contact for all incoming exchange and study abroa

Our services include: advising on employment solutions, best practice, and legislative advice; providing information about pay, reward and benefits, including current salary scales, cost of employment figures, employee benefits, pensions, recognition awards and long service awards; creating, organising and facilitating development events, and providing advice and guidance relating to leadership, management and staff development; providing confidential Occupational Health advice and support on any health issues; widening participation in higher education by attracting a broader variety of students and staff; maintaining HR information systems and data, providing management information to enable analysis and insight to support decision-making, and completing relevant surveys and statutory returns. Whether you have questions about benefits, policies, or you have a general enquiry, the People Hub is there to assist you. In the People Hub you will find: Access to a n

title: Planning Department
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Planning Department Planning Department The Planning Team report to the Vice-Chancellor. We focus on: Strategic Planning Supporting the University's new strategic and financial planning processes. Introducing enhanced approaches to setting KPIs and monitoring the strategic plan. Management Information Providing strategic management information and analysis. Sharing management information through our 'Planning dashboards' using business intelligence software. Liaison across the University's HESA returns to ensure consistency of data. Annual Planning Processes Leading annual planning processes, including student target setting and contributing to budget development. Preparing and monitoring the University's annual Access Agreement. Contributing to development and implementation of the Home/EU recruitment strategy. New entrant target setting and monitoring for Home/EU full-time undergraduates. Bidding Leading teaching-related bids for the University, including the recent successful bid for £5million HEFCE funding to contribute to an £11million project to build a 'Future Technology Centre'. Public Policy Responding to external consultations on public policy both as an institution and as part of a wider group, including the University's response to the Government's call for evidence as part of its 'Review of Post-18 Education and Funding'. Marketing Portal The new Marketing Portal makes it easier for staff to find University images and self-serve designed material including corporate stationery. You’ll find downloadable photography, logos, document and collateral templates, business cards, letterheads and much more. The Portal complies with GDPR legislative requirements. title: Vice-Chancellor's Office
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Vice-Chancellor's Office Vice-Chancellor's Office The Vice-Chancellor leads the University Executive Board (UEB) – which comprises the Vice-Chancellor, Provost, Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Education), Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Global Engagement & Student Life), Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Research, Innovation & External Relations), Chief People Officer, Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director of Corporate Governance, Executive Deans for the Faculties of Business & Law, Creative & Cultural Industries, Humanities & Social Sciences, Science & Health and Technology and the Principal of London Campus. Members' responsibilities‌ foster high performance, and be passionate about achieving results for the University; challenge assumptions and be creative around problems and solutions; recognise the impact of their decisions on students and colleagues; be committed to enabling all staff to contribute to innovative solutions; seek to work with empathy, openness and honesty. The Executive Board will be: characterised by a trusting and enabling mindset; resilient in taking cabinet decisions and seeing them enacted; efficient and effective in managing finances and resources; enabling in its approach to supporting change across the University; committed to ensure that the entire institution is focused on the future direction of the University. The team will normally meet fortnightly to discuss strategy, policy and resourcing and to ensure the University is managed effectively. Home Staff Services Departments Resources Departments What Disability Advice does Disability Advice provides a confidential service for all students with additional learning needs and can advise on the additional academic support available while they study. They can also discuss what reasonable adjustments are required and refer students to other appropriate agencies for support. Students can be advised to contact Disability Advice, in confidence, if they have a condition that might impact on their ability to access the campus, curriculum, teaching, learning, assessments and any other University service. Jump to: Advice for Academic Staff Advice for Faculty Service Centre Managers Advice for Accommodation team, Timetabling team, Fire Officer and Campus Security SITS Training Guidance on Accessible Examination and Assessment Frequently Asked Questions Resources Advice for Departments Academic Staff We have introduced five new reports that you will be able to filter, run and view when you need adjustment and disability information for students that require additional support. These reports are called: Adjustments report for Examination and Assessment (Including in-class tests) Adjustments report for Learning and Teaching Adjustments Report for Timetabling and Access Adjustments report Evacuation Plan Disability Support Needs Report 143 These reports are available to run in SITS and are located on the Disability Advice Page of SITS eVision. Cafe Coco Cafe Coco is based in Gun House, right next to the Student Centre. It provides a warm and friendly atmosphere for staff, students and members of the public all year round. It is decorated in a retro style to give you the atmosphere of days gone by. Our friendly staff serve breakfast from 8.am until 11am, then onto a delicious affordable lunch menu served until 2pm. With healthy Fairtrade eats and sinful treats available along with bean to cup coffee. Enjoy a burger and chip if you prefer. Cafe Coco is also available for use for internal and external meeting bookings, where catering is required. We can provide an informal, relaxed atmosphere. Please contact Cafe Coco on ex 3847 to discuss availability and catering options. Term Time Opening 8.00am - 3.00pm Monday - Friday Chimes Cafe Come and visit our friendly team offering a variety of great food. The venue offers the full Starbucks range of hot drinks, along with a variety of snacks to meet all your needs. Our fantastic breakfast menu offers an extensive range of delicious breakfast items which are served daily until 11.00am. Our bacon butties are great value and will set you up for the day. Term Time Opening from 8.00am - 5.00pm Monday - Thursday, 8.00am - 4.30pm Friday Located on the ground Floor of the City Learning Centre Winston Churchill Avenue. Enjoying a Starbucks drink at campus prices. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pay, Reward and Benefits Human Resources Travel to work As a University, we aim to reduce congestion in Portsmouth and improve the air quality for everyone who studies, lives and works in our city. A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. Sustainable travel Leaving the car at home and making sustainable travel choices can save you time and money. You may also benefit from improved wellbeing and reduce your environmental impact at the same time. Whether you’re looking for information about local cycle routes to work, walking maps or links to public transport you can access it and plan your journey on the My Journey Hampshire website. You can also commute together with others by using easitShare. This scheme covers journeys made by car, bicycle, taxi, motorbikes, train, buses and on foot. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ Explore HR HomepageMy RoleLeaving My RoleManaging My TeamHR Business Partnering TeamLearning and DevelopmentHR SystemsMy Rewards and BenefitsPensionsPayrollOccupational Health ServiceEquality and DiversityContact usStaff Surveys HR A-Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Estates and Campus Services
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Estates and Campus Services > Soft Services (Campus Environment) Estates and Campus Services Visitor car parking Spaces are only reserved for Corporate Business Use via Departments. Staff inviting visitors to the University must make them aware of the University’s parking regulations. Visitors must display a car parking scratch card in their vehicle’s windscreen. Visitors can park either in a reserved visitors’ bay or any standard car parking space available. Reserved bays are for the sole use of visitors on business only – the University has centralised visiting bays in two main areas on the campus, in St Andrew’s Court (This car park is for visitors only staff are not permitted to park) One-day scratch card parking permits can be purchased from the MyPort Information Hub in the Nuffield Centre. Each scratch card is valid only for the day of use and the cost is £2. To make a reservation for a visitor, please email parking.reservations@port.ac.uk with your request, specifying the date, time, location, the name of the visitor and the registration number of their vehicle. All requests for car parking spaces must be made no later than 3pm on the day before the day the reservation is required. Please note: visitor spaces are limited and cannot be guaranteed. Any permit holder may park in a designated visitors’ area as long as they remove their car 30 minutes prior to the arrival time indicated on the A4 post. If a visitor space has not been used after 1 hour of the stated arrival time, the bay reverts to being an available space. Please note: This excludes St Andrew's Court. Students and contractors are not permitted to purchase scratch cards. Please see li

title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > EDI in Action Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Use our breastfeeding and expressing facilities There are two dedicated breastfeeding and expressing facilities on campus. These facilities are designed to provide a comfortable and private space for breastfeeding and expressing milk, and to help ease the transition back to work for new mothers, promote the well-being of both parent and child, and uphold our commitment to celebrate diversity and equal opportunity through an inclusive culture. The two pods feature a comfortable chair with plug sockets and a small table, and can be locked for privacy. Pod location and booking The facilities are bookable and are accessed via a combination lock. The pods are located in: the University Library, on the second floor opposite the lift; Richmond Building, fifth floor. Accessing and using the rooms Locate the key code: the key code for accessing the room is provided in the automatic booking acceptance email Use the key code: enter the key code on the box located on the outside of the pod, then turn the dial to open to access the fob key Hold the key fob to the scanner: once you have the key fob, hold it to the scanner to unlock the door Lock the door: after entering, lock the door from the inside to ensure uninterrupted use Keep the room neat and tidy for the next person, and take any personal items or waste with you after use Return the fob: after using the room, please return the key fob to the key press so it’s ready for the next user If you experience any issues using the rooms, please report these to estates.helpdesk@port.ac.uk. Explore Staff NetworksWhat the Team DoesEDI in Action C

title: The Vice-Chancellor's Awards for Excellence | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> The VC Awards Recognising our staff, alumni and local community Our people are at the heart of our success and our colleagues continue to make exceptional contributions to the University and the wider community. The Vice-Chancellor’s Awards for Excellence recognise and celebrate staff, alumni and members of the community who have made an exceptional contribution to the University, its values, its ambition and its success, as set out in the University Strategy. The awards complement the newly established Student Impact Awards, which recognise the achievements of our students and their contributions to our city and wider region. Awards categories Internationalisation Awarded to an individual or team that demonstrates an outstanding contribution, activity or collaboration that shows the University operating with global vision and ambition. Green Impact Awarded to an individual or team that demonstrates commitment to environmental issues, encompassing innovative approaches and showing clear evidence that the work is making a difference. Outstanding Contribution to Equity, Diversity and Inclusion Awarded to an individual or team initiative through which colleagues and/or students are treated fairly and with respect, and where diversity is championed. Initiatives may focus on colleagues or the student body, or both, and can be either innovative in themselves, or based on implementing ideas or practice from within or outside the University. Outstanding Contribution to Civic Impact and Community Engagement Awarded to an individual or team that has made a significant contribution to aid the wider community, whether socially, culturally or economically. Partnerships, Collaboration & Knowledge Exchange Awarded to an individual or team that has achieved an outstanding partnership with a business or an industry – regiona

title: About us | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> We're a new breed of university Find out how we make a positive difference to the world and how you can join us Welcome to the University of Portsmouth We’re proud to be a breath of fresh air in the academic world – a place where everyone gets the support they need to achieve their best. We’re always discovering. We don’t fit the mould, we break it. Just as the world keeps moving, so do we. We’re closely involved with our local community and we take our ideas into the global marketplace. We partner with business, industry and government to set the course for a better future. And we educate and transform the lives of our students to help them achieve their potential. We stand out, not just in the UK but in the world. We excel in research areas from cosmology and astrophysics to cyber security, forensics and sport science. Since the first day we opened our doors, we've looked towards the future. And we're here to help you shape it. Our story Our Strategy Our facilities Our ambition Structure and governance Key dates Working at Portsmouth Contact us Our London campus Study in London at the University of Portsmouth, in the heart of Waltham Forest. Our purpose-built, capital city campus puts world-class teaching and industry connections on your doorstep, allowing you to fuel your ambition in one of the world's greatest financial centres. Discover more You may also be interested in Policies and standards Freedom of Information Publications Our staff Maps and directions | We use cookiesWe may use cookies to analyse visitor data, improve our website, show personalised content and give you a great website experience. For more information about cookies on our site and to tailor the cookies we collect, select ‘Manage settings’.Accept all cookiesDeny optional cookiesManage settings

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) Supporting the mental health and wellbeing of all our colleagues is incredibly important to us, and our new Employee Assistance Programme will help us do just that. Whether you’re facing problems at work or at home, you can access impartial, independent and entirely confidential advice from qualified counsellors for any issues you may be facing, such as Anxiety; Bereavement; Stress; Depression; Workplace Issues; Trauma; Relationships; Finances and debt; and Family Difficulties. Accessing the Helpline Confidential 24/7 Helpline: The telephone helpline is available 24/7, 365 days a year and is completely confidential and to anyone needing mental health support. To access the helpline, simply call 0800 023 9324 from any standard UK landline or mobile phone Digital Resources and Self-Help:A wide offering of resources is also available and includes downloadable self-help workbooks, debt advice and more. Visit the EAP Platform to find out more: www.port.ac.uk/myeap Is it confidential? The University does not know who uses the service unless the individual personally chooses to tell someone about their contact with Vivup. Vivup do provide statistics to the University to show how many employees use the service and the broad types of issues raised, for example; ‘relationship breakdown at home’ or ‘bullying and harassment in the workplace. No information is ever passed on which could potentially identify an individual. Explore HR HomepageMy Role My Responsibilities My Benefits Portsmouth FC Mortgage Advice Car Benefit Scheme Holiday Purchase Scheme My Pay Leave and Absence Managing My C

title: Dental Academy Patient Services | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Operated under an NHS contract and located in the University's Dental Academy, our dental practice is where our dental students can provide high-quality dental care to patients, for free, under the supervision of our qualified staff. Patient care Our student dental teams are skilled at their work but operate slower than qualified professionals, so appointments can take longer than expected. You can access our dental services if: You don't currently have a dentist You're available during the day for long appointments, typically around 1.5 hours You can come to multiple appointments Treatments you can get as one of our patients: Fillings and preventative treatment Gum treatment Crowns and bridgework Dentures Root canal fillings Oral health promotion Patient applications Please visit our page on the NHS website to find out if we're currently accepting patients. Supervised by our qualified staff, our students in the clinic offer free dental treatment Based within our Dental Academy, our clinic is a modern dental practice where our students develop their dentistry skills Opening hours and contact information The Dental Academy is open 8.30am – 5.00pm Monday to Thursday, and until 4.00pm on Fridays. We are closed on weekends. You can contact the Dental Academy at 023 9284 5285. Patient satisfaction We're continually improving our patient services. At the end of each course of treatment we encourage our patients to share their experiences with us. We ask our patients for their views and opinions through our patient satisfaction surveys, NHS Friends and Family Test (FFT), and our comments book to see where we can improve. Quarterly reports Patient comments and satisfaction ratings are collected and put together in bi-annual reports which are shared with all staff, students and NHS England. These reports can also be

title: The Chaplaincy | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Our Chaplaincy Confidential support, whatever your religion or beliefs Life can be full of surprises and sometimes it can be difficult to cope. Whatever troubles you face whilst at uni – a sudden change in circumstances, a loss of some kind or a tough decision – our University Chaplains can offer support, guidance and a willing ear. The Chaplaincy Centre and Prayer Rooms can be found on St Pauls Road. It is open to all students and staff for a confidential chat, somewhere to have lunch with friends, as a place to pray or just find some quiet space. We'll listen to you confidentially about whatever you want to explore. Our Chaplains have pastoral skills that can help with establishing values, meaning and purpose for living. The Chaplaincy Centre & Prayer Rooms are now open with the following times: Monday – Thursday: 9.00am – 5.00pm Friday: 9.00am – 4.00pm This area is a great place to get away and to meet new friends. If you need a talk, this is the place. If you want to join a religious society, this is the place. I enjoy this place because the atmosphere is stress-free and you can always talk to staff. 2nd year student, Anonymous What is the Chaplaincy? The Chaplaincy is a safe and inclusive social space. We're open to all students, regardless of your values and beliefs. Free tea and coffee is available every day during the week. We offer: Confidential support – drop in and ask to see a Chaplain for a one-to-one chat, or to make an appointment email us at chaplains@port.ac.uk Islamic Prayer Rooms – now open in the Chaplaincy Centre in St Paul's Road, for Muslim staff and students, with separate ablution facilities for Brothers and Sisters Multi-faith Room - a quiet space for reflection, stillness and personal prayer, open to everyone Events - throughout the year, including Mug Painting during Freshers Week

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Display Screen Equipment (DSE) Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Introduction Display Screen Equipment (DSE) are devices such as screen monitors, laptops, touch screens and other similar devices. Overuse or improper use of DSE can cause fatigue, eye strain, upper limb problems and backache to staff or students. These problems can also be experienced from poorly designed workstations or work environments. The causes may not always be obvious and can be due to a combination of factors. The use of DSE is governed by the Health and Safety (Display Screen Equipment) Regulations. In order to comply with these regulations, the University of Portsmouth provides employees with: An eye test if a worker requires one (habitual users) Display Screen Equipment awareness training and assessment Training and information for workers Occupational Health Referrals Defining DSE Users The DSE regulations define a user as an individual who habitually uses display screen equipment as a significant part of their normal work. As a guide, an individual is considered a user if they have sessions of continuous DSE use for more than 1 hour nearly every day. A large amount of display screen equipment work, particularly that carried out by academics and other researchers, is intermittent. Therefore in these cases, the total time spent using display screen equipment over a period of a week should be considered and the following guide used: If the average is about 2 hours a day or more, the individual is considered a user If the average is less than 1 hour then, subject to an examination of the type of usage, the individual is not defined as a user If the average is between 1 and 2 hours, the type of usage must be taken into account Request an eye test Members of staff ca

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources University of Portsmouth will no longer use our existing provider for discounts. My Discounts will therefore no longer be managed by Sodexo moving forwards. Employees will no longer have access to the site as of 01/10/23. Please see the below FAQ’s to help with any queries you may have: I have purchased unused e-vouchers; how do I access these once the site is closed? We suggest all employees download their e vouchers prior to 01/10/23. Employees who have not been able to do this can email us at employeebenefits@sodexoengage.com and we will provide employees with the link manually by email. I have purchased unused cinema e-codes. How do I access these once the scheme is closed? We suggest all employees download their ecodes prior to the 01/10/23. Employees who have not been able to do this can email us at employeebenefits@sodexoengage.com and we will provide employees with the link manually by email. Am I able to reload my reloadable card after the scheme is closed? No, but funds will remain on cards to drawdown, retailer T&Cs and expiry date still apply. I have an existing holiday booking; what are the implications for me after the scheme has closed? There will be no impact for employees that already have a booking with the travel team. They will have our contact details and reference numbers on their paperwork so will be able to contact us directly. Call 01908 303531 or email travel.uk@sodexo.com and our dedicated travel team will be able to help. Can I use my spree card after the scheme has closed? Employees will be able to use their Spree cards until their card expires (2 years). Any cashback outstanding – y

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Car Benefit Scheme The Car Benefit Scheme is brought to you by CPC Drive in partnership with Tusker, and is a new way for University employees to run a car. A fixed monthly amount is taken directly from your gross salary and in return you get the use of a brand new electric or plug-in hybrid car. The reduction to your gross salary is made before income tax and National Insurance (NI) although, under the scheme you will need to pay Benefit in Kind as the car is being provided as a benefit by your employer. There are additional savings available on pure electric vehicles as Benefit in Kind is set at just 2% until April 2025, increasing by 1% each year until April 2028. You can choose a car from a number of leading manufacturers and every car comes complete with: Maintenance of the vehicle including all servicing, batteries, exhausts and tyres; Fully comprehensive motor insurance including all business travel for you; Lifestyle protections; Annual road tax; Roadside assistance (homestart & recovery UK & European cover); Relief car for when your car is off the road (if selected). What’s more, there is no deposit needed to secure a car on this scheme. Eligibility In order to access the car benefit scheme, you must: be a permanent employee or on a fixed term contract where your contract of employment is for a longer period than the proposed Salary Sacrifice agreement; have completed a minimum of 6 months service and not be in receipt of any warnings under the disciplinary policy; and your gross salary after taking into account all salary sacrifice arrangements must be above the National Minimum Wage. Access the scheme

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Holiday Purchase Scheme The Holiday Purchase Scheme application window for the 2025/26 leave year is now closed. 2025/26 Holiday Purchase Timeline Wednesday 16 July to Friday 22 August - Application Window Open Sunday 31 August - Management Review and Approvals Final Deadline Friday 12 September - Deadline for HR letter confirming costs and terms to be sent to applicant via ESS Tuesday 30 September - Final deadline for applicant to sign agreement letter and confirm payment option in ESS Scheme Summary The Holiday Purchase Scheme allows eligible employees to apply to purchase up to 5 days (37 hours) additional time off (pro-rata for part-time employees) in a leave year, over and above existing entitlements to holidays, public holidays and University closure days. The scheme: Provides flexibility to achieve the best possible balance between work and personal commitments Ensures a clear, equitable, and consistent approach to considering applications, and outlines the circumstances in which an application may be declined under management review Permits the cost to be spread over the entire leave year via a salary sacrifice arrangement (reducing costs of tax and national insurance), or optionally a non-salary sacrifice arrangement deducted after tax, national insurance and other statutory deductions have already been made Does not permit the selling of untaken or unwanted annual leave Is available to salaried permanent and fixed-term employees whose contract extends beyond the end of the upcoming leave year Please read the Holiday Purchase Scheme Guide in full prior to undertaking any application or management review

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Free mortgage education, advice and brokerage services A new partnership with Charles Cameron & Associates (CC&A) provides all university colleagues with mortgage education, advice, and brokerage services for free. Whether you are a first-time buyer looking to re-mortgage or finance an investment property, they can help you access the best possible financial solutions for your needs. Their services include: Educational webinars tailored for all life and purchase stages; Free, flexible video or telephone meetings with a dedicated adviser; Full application support; 24/7 mortgage information, available via an online tool; Affordability calculator and downloadable report; CC&A Mortgage Pledge – a mortgage tracking service for all existing homeowners to ensure you are never paying more for your mortgage than you need to; Topical newsletters and buyer guides. The Benefits of their services are: Tailored mortgage advice for First Time Buyers, Home Movers, Re-mortgagers and Buy-To-Let Landlords, including Equity Release, Commercial and Bridging Loans; Access to preferential rates unavailable to customers directly; Totally independent and whole of market advice; Completely Broker Fee Free for both yourself and your family & friends; Sustainability pledge – CC&A will plant a tree for every mortgage arranged. Not only will CC&A source the best possible mortgage product for your circumstances, but they will also save you time by taking care of all the paperwork involved – chasing the lender, surveyor and conveyancers, then updating you all the way through to completion, making the whole process as stress-free as possible. T

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Tuition fees discount The University offers a 30% discount to self-funding, Student Loans Company funded and externally sponsored University of Portsmouth staff members undertaking part-time HE3 courses. Eligible employees should email income@port.ac.uk to apply for the staff discount, no more than four weeks prior to the course start date. Application is necessary for each year of registration. The discount is only available for the duration of employment with the University. The University also offers a 30% discount to children of University staff* members undertaking undergraduate and postgraduate taught, full-time and part-time study. The discount is available to self-funding, Student Loans Company funded and externally sponsored students who are studying programmes delivered by the University of Portsmouth. The discount is not applicable to students studying franchised or collaborative provision. Children of University staff are defined as biological, step or adopted, aged under 25 on 31 August in each academic year of registration. Eligible employees/students should enquire by email at: income@port.ac.uk. Note that the discount cannot be fully confirmed until the student is registered on the course due to the criteria set for staff eligibility. This application for discount does not affect the UCAS application process. Applications for funding via Student Loans Company should be made quoting the full course fee. The University will submit a change request to reduce the liability when the discount is confirmed. The detailed conditions and how to apply are set out in the Tuition Fee Discounts Policy (pdf). *

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Portsmouth Football Club - Free and Discounted Tickets Free Tickets Through its partnership with Portsmouth Football Club, the University has access to spectator ticket seating at Fratton Park for each of the club's men's first team home matches. For the 2023/24 season, the University has launched a staff-wide ballot for these free of charge spectator seats at Fratton Park.The allocation is for stadium seats only. No travel or wider in-stadium benefits are offered by the club for these seats. Staff can apply for tickets via this Google Form. You can apply for a range of men's first team matches running from 29th July 2023 to 20 April 2024 to either attend as a work group (up to six tickets per request), which could be colleagues, students or external partners, or to take a friend/ family member as a plus one (two tickets per request). Tickets will be drawn at random for each match and staff will be notified by email if they are successful. Ballots will be done in two month intervals to ensure staff have the chance to enter throughout the season. E-tickets will then be emailed out prior to each match date. Discounted Tickets If you're unsuccessful in the ballot or want to be sure of seeing a match, as a member of staff you can watch Pompey in action at Fratton Park for a discounted price of £16. To book, first register with the Pompey e-ticket site then, once logged in, click on this link to access the £16 discounted University of Portsmouth tickets. Once you’ve selected your seat in your match of choice, scroll down the Price Class Options to find the University of Portsmouth ticket. And remember to take proof o

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pensions Human Resources Your Pensions & What you Need to Know This section directs you to the information you need to know about your pension, what it is worth to you, and what it means to be a member. We aim to point you to the right information throughout your membership, from starting your career to preparing for life in retirement. We have employees in a range of pension schemes, depending on how you are employed, when you were employed, and what role(s) you hold. Follow the appropriate links below for further information (if you aren't sure what scheme you are in, check your contract or payslip): Local Government Pension Scheme (LGPS) - available for University of Portsmouth employees in Support, Administrative, Professional Services who started prior to August 2024 and all non-teaching Research roles Teachers' Pension Scheme (TPS) - available to University of Portsmouth employees in academic teaching roles who are expected to undertake research. Teaching roles with no research duties, such as Teaching Fellows, are enrolled MyPensionPlus instead (see below) MyPensionPlus - for permanent and fixed term employees of UoP Academic Services Limited (UASL) Portsmouth from August 2024 UASL NEST - for casual, zero-hours and hourly paid workers at UoP Academic Services Limited (UASL) Portsmouth from August 2024 NHS Pension Scheme - new employees in eligible healthcare roles may be able to remain in their NHS Pension if they are on a medical register and were in NHS scheme employment prior to joining Portsmouth Aviva for London Campus - for UoP Academic Services Limited (UASL) London campus employees only NEST (Other Subsidiaries) - availa

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pay, Reward and Benefits Human Resources Pay, reward and benefits This section provides information about grading of roles, employee benefits and pensions. Employee benefits Information about the range of benefits and entitlements available to current members of University staff. This is also available under My Role. Pensions Information and links to the schemes available to University employees can be found here. Life Assurance The three main occupational pension schemes (TP, LGPS and NHS) provide death in service cover to members for three times their salary. In order to support staff who have decided to opt out of the occupational pension schemes, the University has put in place equivalent life assurance cover. Staff covered by this policy will be contacted in writing. For further details about the policy cover, and terms and conditions, please refer to the Registered Policy Document. If you have opted out of one of the occupational pension schemes because you have reached the Lifetime Allowance, please refer to the Excepted Scheme Policy Document. Please note, you will need to raise a ticket via People Hub, with a copy of the HMRC certificate to confirm Lifetime Allowance protection before this cover can be provided. Group Life cover is provided by the University on a discretionary basis. The provision of the cover will be reviewed on an annual basis. Grading of Roles The University is committed to equal pay for work of equal value for all employees and ensures compliance with the Equal Pay and the Equality Act 2010. Trained role analysts within HR, evaluate the level of responsibility and competence required to do each job using t

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Leave and absence For information on Leave and Absence please follow this link. The information below provides information on leave and absence options, with links to relevant policies and guidance. Some may be paid/unpaid/annual leave or combination thereof; and some also may be subject to eligibility/qualifying periods, therefore the full policy should be referred to for actual details. Annual leave The annual leave year for all staff runs from 1 October to 30 September in any year and staff are expected to take their entitlement within this timeframe. Leave entitlement Academic and Research staff receive an annual leave entitlement of 35 days per year (259 hours) per year. Professional Services staff receive an annual leave entitlement of 32 days (236.8 hours) per year. Part-time staff have a pro-rata entitlement to leave based on the number of hours/weeks they work (their fte). Temporary staff on variable hours contracts have an entitlement to 32 days per annum pro rata and this will be calculated in proportion to the number of hours worked. You can check your leave entitlement, balance and leave taken by viewing your Self Service record, which you can access directly via Employee Self-service (ESS). You can also access Self Service from the Staff Essentials page. Joining or leaving the University - leave entitlement Holiday entitlement for part-years on starting (and leaving) is calculated on complete days of service. On leaving employment at the University, any outstanding leave entitlement to date of leaving should be taken. If (in exceptional circumstances and as agreed with the line manager) this is not

A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. Sustainable travel Leaving the car at home and making sustainable travel choices can save you time and money. You may also benefit from improved wellbeing and reduce your environmental impact at the same time. Whether you’re looking for information about local cycle routes to work, walking maps or links to public transport you can access it and plan your journey on the My Journey Hampshire website. You can also commute together with others by using easitShare. This scheme covers journeys made by car, bicycle, taxi, motorbikes, train, buses and on foot. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ Explore HR HomepageMy RoleLeaving My RoleManaging My TeamHR Business Partnering TeamLearning and DevelopmentHR SystemsMy Rewards and BenefitsPensionsPayrollOccupational Health ServiceEquality and DiversityContact usStaff Surveys HR A-Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources My responsibilities The information below explains the expectations the University has about the behaviour and actions of staff members. Personal information The University needs to process personal information about you as a staff member in order to carry out necessary administration, this includes your home address, emergency contact details and current bank details It is the responsibility of each member of staff to ensure that your personal information is kept up-to-date. You can do this via Employee Self Service (ESS). Probation period When joining the University, new employees will be given a probationary period. This is a formal structured procedure aimed at assessing and reviewing the employee's performance, capability and suitability for the role. The probation process is intended to support you in your new role and to highlight training and development needs or measures required to help you to fulfil your new responsibilities. Declaration of interests The University’s Declaration of Interests Policy states that all staff must make a declaration of interests if, having read the policy, they find that they have an actual or potential conflict of interest between their private interests and their employment by the University. If you need to make a declaration of interest then please complete the online form (please note that you will need to be logged into your University Gmail account in order to access the form). My performance The University provides support and development to ensure that people can achieve their best and play their part in meeting the goals and objectives of their team in a way that i

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources My Pay Salary scales Your salary will be paid monthly in arrears on the last working day of each month (apart from December, when it is paid earlier) by credit transfer to your bank account. The University issues electronic payslips to staff which can be viewed on Employee Self Service (ESS). If you need to change the details of the bank account your salary is paid into, you must complete and submit a Change of Bank Account Details form. You can view the University of Portsmouth current Salary Scales listing all spine points and grades. Progression to the top of your grade is by annual increment. The Real Living Wage The University has made a commitment to the real Living Wage set by the Living Wage Foundation. The current rate for the real Living Wage outside of London is £13.45 per hour. Timesheets, claims and expenses All hourly paid employees including Casual employees, Zero Hours Workers and Academic employees must claim for their worked hours via Paid Time which is accessible through Employee Self Service. You will be paid on a monthly basis on the last working day of the month and all claims should be submitted as soon as the work has taken place. All staff members should be reimbursed in full for all types of properly authorised expenses incurred in the course of the University’s business. The regulations and procedure for claiming expenses are described in the University’s Expenses Manual. Each month, forms and claims must be submitted by the relevant Payroll deadlines*. Reward & Recognition Recognition Awards - Under the Reset programme, Recognition Awards are currently suspended. *Internal only Explor

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Managing my contract The information below provides useful information and advice on aspects of your contract. Changing my working pattern All employees have the chance to request a change to their contractual terms and conditions of employment to work more flexibly, by making a Flexible Work Request, which is part of our range of family-friendly entitlements*. This could be a change to your working pattern, or perhaps the number of hours you work. It is important to note that this is the right to request a change only, and not the automatic right to actually change your pattern. Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) checks A DBS check is required for individuals are working in certain professions, or with children or vulnerable adults. The University’s Human Resources (HR) department is responsible for administering Disclosure and Barring Service checks with the UK Government’s Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS), for staff where they are required for employment purposes, as dictated by UK legislation. The HR department also administers DBS checks for staff who need to apply for a Research Passport. Research Passports A Research Passport is the mechanism for non-NHS staff (i.e Higher Education institution employees who have no contractual arrangement with the NHS) to obtain an Honorary Research Contract or Letter of Access when they propose to carry out research in the NHS.
