body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Developing my career The University of Portsmouth is committed to providing a programme of good quality leadership, management and staff development to ensure that you understand your role in delivering University ambitions, and have the skills you need to be fully effective within your role. My Career and development goals Staff are encouraged to develop skills and self-awareness at work to contribute to their career development, find out more about the Career Development Framework. The University of Portsmouth Staff Mentoring Scheme Mentoring is a supportive opportunity to aid staff in identifying and achieving career goals. It supports the development of a culture of equality and diversity and contributes to our Athena SWAN and HR Excellence in Research award commitment. Find out more about the University Staff Mentoring Scheme. Development opportunities The University offers various development opportunities for staff and support for leaders and managers. Learning and Development offer a two-day event entitled Developing your Supervisory Skills. This is aimed primarily at those aspiring or new to the role of first line supervisor. The event is participative and designed to develop practical supervisory management skills and confidence in the role. A full range of Learning & Development workshops are available, just click on the links in the calendar of events to find out more. The Personal Development Review (PDR) We all benefit from taking time out to think about how our work is going and what we are trying to achieve. The Personal Development Review (PDR) process offers a chance to discuss your own thought

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Ensuring my health, safety and wellbeing The University of Portsmouth is committed to ensuring the health, safety and wellbeing of its employees. Covid-19 Pandemic - Working from home safely Given the current Covid-19 Coronavirus Pandemic, it has been necessary to develop Remote Working Resources to help staff and managers to support staff as we move to home working. This guidance covers working safely from home and remote working via laptop, desktop PC or paper-based. Occupational Health The University’s Occupational Health Service provides advice to individuals and managers on work related health problems and health problems that can affect work. Our Occupational Health pages also contain information and advice on Mental Health. Safety The University considers that a strong health, safety and wellbeing culture together with a safe and secure environment are fundamental to its success. The Health & Safety office provides information on all aspects of health and safety within the University, which includes accident prevention and reporting, first-aid provision, workplace inspections and the Health & Safety training events calendar. To find out more, visit the Health and Safety Policy webpage where you can download the Health & Safety Policy Statement and the Health & Safety Policy Organisation & Arrangements. Travelling overseas on University business Without exception, overseas travel must be formally approved in principle by the Head of Department and/or Line Manager. This is by means of authorised FIN1 (staff) or FIN30 (student). If the trip is being financed by an external organisation and therefore does not

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Dealing with change Change is something that everyone experiences. By understanding how you might be affected, and by preparing appropriately, you can adapt to and benefit from these changes successfully. Online resources: The Change Curve How Does This Change Make Me Feel? Additional Support: Our Occupational Health Department can advise and support where an individual’s health and well-being may be affected by change. The Employee Assistance Programme can offer you support, information and advice on a range of change issues, you can also call and talk in confidence to one of their telephone Helpline advisors. The Organisational Change, Redeployment and Redundancy Policy (OCRR) sets out the University’s approach to dealing with organisational change that may lead to a reduction in employee numbers. Change management and Delivering successful change The Change Management section of the online Learning & Development Toolkit is packed with professional development materials designed to assist you in dealing with the day-to-day challenges of your job. University Negotiating and Consultative Committee (UNCC) "The University Negotiating and Consultative Committee (UNCC) is the forum for all consultation and negotiations at the University and comprises representatives from both management and trades unions." The trades unions recognised by the University of Portsmouth are UNISON representing support staff and UCU representing academic and research members of staff. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > HR Business Partnering Team Human Resources HR Business Partnering Team The HR Business Partnering (HRBP) Team provides professional and proactive advice and guidance on HR policies and procedures. We support full case management and consultations on employment-related matters ensuring consistency of employment practice across the University. The HRBP Team are dedicated Human Resources professionals providing strategic support for University leadership. Partnered with the University Executive and their extended teams, we offer guidance to enable the delivery of key people initiatives, which in turn maximises organisational performance and we are focussed on the delivery of strategic HR, supporting the embedding and implementation of the People Strategy, ensuring HR resource and practice is aligned to the University’s strategic and operational requirements. The HRBP Team comprises Senior HR Business Partners, Strategic HR Business Partners, Employee Relations Advisers, Employee Relations Officer and a HR Coordinator. Contact details and a full summary of representatives for each Faculty / Professional Service can be located on our Contact Us page. Change Bank Account Details If you want to update your bank account details that your salary is paid in, you will be able to do this within Employee Self-Service. For support on how to complete a bank change visit the HR Systems, Employee Self-Service site. Claims Submission If you have been appointed on either a Casual Employee or Zero Hours contract, you will be required to claim your hours via Paid Time, accessible through Employee Self Service. Training on Paid Time, including user guides, will be provided to you by your line manager. External Worker Pay Claims Submission The below SAL forms are to be used by the External Worker to claim their fee and, where allowed, any incurred expenses. Please ensure you select the relevant claim for the engagement being undertaken. SAL5 - External Visiting Lecturer claim SAL6 - External Examiner claim SAL8 - External Assessor claim Paid Time Hours Authorisation If changes are required to your Paid Time authorisation group, for instance, staff to be removed or added, send details of your change to Payroll by submitting a ticket via the HR People Hub. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Equality and Diversity Human Resources Equality and diversity The University is dedicated to ensuring equality of opportunity and the promotion of diversity and inclusion for the benefit of our staff and students. We welcome students to Portsmouth from over 120 countries each year, and we are committed to widening participation in higher education across an ever-increasing variety of students - and indeed staff. The Equality and Diversity Unit owns the policies, guidance and University Equality Objectives that outline the way in which we understand and accommodate the needs of students and staff. We also produce annual comprehensive data reports detailing the University's performance on key areas and their impacts on the staff and student experience. Read more about the Equality and Diversity unit. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Staff Surveys Human Resources Staff Surveys The University is committed to seeking the views of staff on the quality of their working life, and suggestions by staff on the ways in which the University can change and improve to ensure the continued and successful delivery of the University’s Strategy. One of the ways this is achieved is through the staff survey. Staff surveys help senior leadership understand how staff feel about working at the University. Staff surveys also provide an opportunity for staff to gather ideas and work together to improve staff development opportunities, our University environment and, ultimately, the services we provide to students, the community and our partners. Inclusive Culture Survey 2022 People Insight Ltd were commissioned to undertake the University’s 2022 inclusive culture survey as an external independent provider. The University chose to partner with People Insight to ensure that staff had the opportunity to share their honest opinions about their experiences of working at the University through a confidential survey process. The survey was conducted between 31 January and 18 February 2022 and achieved an overall response rate of 52%. 3,301 staff members were invited to take part in the survey and 1,725 responded. This was up from a response rate of 31% for our previous gender culture survey (when 986 staff responded). This report provides a top level summary of the survey responses. Included are comparisons to the like for like questions (where available) from 2018 when the last gender culture survey was conducted. Further comparisons are provided through People Insight’s Higher Education Insti

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Conditions of employment Human Resources Other absence and leave In this section, you will find information about jury and witness service, reservist duties (service in non-regular forces) and time off for public duties. Jury Service Employees may be required at any time to undertake a period of jury service. The court will pay the juror an allowance for financial loss and expenses. The University does not make any deductions from pay in respect of absence on account of jury service. The court will issue a form to a juror which should be forwarded to the Payroll Office. A financial loss claim is not necessary. Please raise a ticket through the People Hub so this leave can be added to iTrent. Witness Service A procedure similar to that of Jury Service operates for employees undertaking witness service. The Criminal Prosecution Service will issue a form to a witness to claim compensation for expenses. A financial loss claim is not necessary. Please raise a ticket through the People Hub so this leave can be added to iTrent. Reservist Duties Staff who are volunteer members of the non-Regular Forces, eg Territorial Army, and who attend summer camp are eligible for 2 weeks additional paid leave irrespective of their normal leave. This leave will be recorded on the HR System by HR Department staff. Occasionally, staff are called up for active duty and in these circumstances, you should contact your line manager and your HR Business Partner/HR Adviser immediately. Time Off for Public Duties Employees are allowed ‘reasonable’ time off during working hours for the performance of the following duties: a Magistrate a Justice of the Peace a member

Home Staff Services Departments Resources Association of University Administrators - University of Portsmouth Branch The AUA is the professional body for managers and administrators working in HE in the UK and Ireland. It was formed from a merger of CUA and APA (Conference of University Administrators and Association of Polytechnic Administrators) when the Polytechnics acquired university status, so has a history dating back to the 1960s and 70s. Its aim is to promote excellence in HE management and administration and it is committed to raising the profile of management and administration in HE as a profession, developing best practice and high standards in the area. Please visit the University of Portsmouth AUA pages at www.port.ac.uk/aua. © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Estates and Campus Services
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Estates and Campus Services > Soft Services (Campus Environment) Estates and Campus Services University Bus Service The University runs two bus services to campus from Monday to Friday during term-time. The U1 takes a circular route around Portsmouth, and the U2 goes to the Langstone sports site. The bus is primarily for student use, however staff can use it for university activities when there is capacity for a charge. In order to use the bus, you’ll need to download the First Bus app via the Play Store or Apple store to purchase digital tickets. You can either buy a day ticket priced at £3 or a calendar month-long ticket for £45. Alternatively, a day ticket can be bought for £3 when boarding the bus. The U1 circular route runs with stops at Bransbury Park in Eastney, Prince Albert Road, Francis Avenue, Fratton Bridge, Somers Road, Winston Churchill Avenue, Cambridge Road, Elm Grove, Kings Theatre, Festing Road and Eastney Health Centre. Buses run every 15 minutes during weekdays in term time. The U1 bus doesn't run at weekends, during the academic holidays or on public holidays. For more information and for up to date timetables view our University Buses page on MyPort. The U2 bus stops at Spinnaker Building, University Learning Centre, Fratton Bridge, Prince Albert Road and Langstone Campus. The service also stops at all First bus stops along the route (only times for the main stops listed above are shown in the U2 timetable). The U2 bus doesn't run on weekends, during the academic holidays or on public holidays. About the service: Staff will be charged £3 per day or £45 for a monthly ticket Free for students Scan your digital ticket as you board the

Home Staff Services Departments Resources Harassment, bullying and intimidation View the Anti Harassment and Anti Bullying Policy The University is committed to tackling all forms of bullying and harassment and other unacceptable behaviours and ensuring that we treat each other with dignity and respect. The Policy gives a clear direction to all and will help us to continue to value all members of our community by providing a fair, supportive and discrimination-free environment that promotes equality of opportunity for staff, students and others and values diversity. University Harassment Adviser Network A network of harassment advisers is available to help all staff who feel they may have been bullied or have themselves been accused of bullying. The advisers will inform you of your options and offer support, they will never tell you what to do. That decision is always yours. All discussions with a harassment adviser are confidential, and brief records of their discussions will be recorded only for statistical purposes. This information is anonymous and will be held in complete confidence. If you would like a confidential discussion please contact any of the advisers below. body: undefinedUniversity of Portsmouth:CoursesWhy Portsmouth?Application, Fees & FundingResearchUseful:University HomeStudentsStaffSearch HomeSerious incident Serious incident The University has a Major Incident Management Team that will enact the Major Incident Action Plan should an incident occur. Should a serious incident occur there will be information updates, about the frequency and any actions needed, on the University of Portsmouth website. In case of emergency on campus, dial ext. 3333 for University Security or, off campus call 023 9284 3333.

title: Staff Essentials
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Staff Essentials Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. Home Staff Services Departments Resources The University values its employees and their expertise and talent, and understands the impact they have in achieving its Strategy and contributing to its continued success. As such, the University recognises the importance of providing appropriate learning and development opportunities to ensure all employees are committed, motivated, competent and developed in their jobs, with staff working in partnership at all levels. Staff development provision across the University is delivered by a range of Providers: Learning and Development Centre for Academic and Digital Innovation (CADI) Research and Innovation (RIS) Health & Safety IT Training University Library Online Learning and Development platforms: Docebo - the University’s Learning Management System (LMS). Designed to offer an engaging user experience, Docebo enables colleagues to access eLearning, register for events, and explore a wide range of learning and development resources. Visit Docebo to complete all Core and Role-specific training. LinkedIn Learning - Equips you with the tools to excel in your current role, explore new career paths, and stay ahead of industry trends. © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Staff Essentials
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Staff Essentials Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. Home Staff Services Departments Resources Professional Support Staff Association The Professional Support Staff Association (PSSA) is the body that represents all University support staff and in doing so provides them with a voice across the University by representing their interests on a variety of sub-committees and groups. It also provides a means whereby support staff can express their views and opinions to the Board of Governors, Academic Council and the Vice-Chancellor. The PSSA also aims to help promote and foster a spirit of cooperation between its members. There are more than 1,300 professional support staff working across the University in very diverse roles. As a member of professional support staff you are automatically a member of the PSSA and have the power to vote at general meetings. We are always interested in hearing your views and opinions on any subject related to your working life. We are also very keen to hear from any member interested in getting involved in the work of the PSSA. If you would like to contact us please email pssa@port.ac.uk. You can find out more about the PSSA by visiting our site: PSSA Google Site (internal access only) The University's Report and Support should be used to report incidents of socially unacceptable behaviours (this is available to both students and staff). Once you have made contact with this service, support will be provided by either an HR Adviser in the Employment Practice Team or from an Harassment Adviser. Details of the Harassment Adviser Network can be found here. Further information on relevant University policies

title: Working at Portsmouth | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Working at the University of Portsmouth We recruit the best people to deliver the best experience for our students Working with us is rewarding in more ways than one We are a new breed of university in a buzzing, diverse city with plenty of stories to tell, and it's not just our students who enjoy the benefits. We've been awarded an Athena SWAN silver award for our commitment to gender equality, and we've got plenty of other awards and rankings successes to shout about too – but first and foremost, we're here to create bigger and better opportunities for all. We're building a brighter future and we're looking for people to help us get there. If that sounds like something you'd love to do, find out how to apply for a role with us today. Current vacancies Explore our latest external and internal job vacancies here at the University of Portsmouth, and apply for a new role at a top UK university. Read more Microsoft 365 digital transformation We're looking for talented people to help us become a digitally enabled and empowered university through M365, within a variety of permanent and fixed-term roles. Read more Visiting scholars and researchers We're a truly global university and we welcome visiting academics, scholars and researchers from institutions around the world. Read more Staff benefits Discover the financial, personal and social benefits available when you start a new role at the University of Portsmouth – including staff discounts, pension schemes, generous holiday allowances and more. Read more How to apply for a role Find out how to apply for a job at the University of Portsmouth, how we find the right person for each role and what to expect if we invite you for interview. The Document Warehouse is a central store of all University policies, procedures, guidelines and strategies and should be linked to by staff and students from the rest of the website, in order to avoid duplication of documents. The Freedom of Information site has been created in response to the 2000 Act of the same name, which states that the University, as a public authority, must make available a large amount of information to the public. The site contains links to information produced and held by departments throughout the University and is divided into the classes recommended by the Act. title: Academic Registry
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Academic Registry Academic Registry About Us and Who to Contact Welcome to the Academic Registry! The Academic Registry plays a central role in our institution’s commitment to maintaining academic excellence. Our role is to develop and support an academic quality regulatory landscape which supports the highest standards of academic quality, through academic regulations and policies that support a fair, transparent, and effective learning environment. Our Mission Our Mission is to ensure the University’s academic quality environment complies with mandatory regulatory requirements, upholds academic standards, enhances the student experience, and supports all students, regardless of their background, to succeed. Our Values Academic Excellence: Promoting the highest standards of education and quality of student experience Integrity: Ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability in all our actions. Collaboration: Partnering with students, staff and external stakeholders to uphold and improve academic quality. Responsiveness: Adapting to the changing landscape of higher education through innovation and foresight. Our Objectives Working collaboratively across the University, and informed by sector leading-practice we will: Ensure all academic policies, regulations and procedures meet mandatory regulatory requirements whilst being efficient and maintaining a forward-thinking approach. Provide an assessment regulatory framework which supports student achievement, and eliminates unwarranted barriers to learning and success; Create a student and staff-centric academic quality environment that promotes high standards and continuous improvement; Generate and a

title: Financial statements and annual report | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Every academic year, our Board of Governors publishes an audit of our financial statements. This is to make sure our financial management is accurate and robust. This publication is a yearly overview of our financial performance including statements on income and expenditure, the university balance sheet, changes in reserves and cash flow. Our financial strategy We have a robust financial strategy that delivers a successful and financially sustainable university. Over recent years, we've invested heavily in our estates and will continue to do so. And continue to deploy our resources efficiently. We will plan our capital investment more effectively to derive maximum strategic benefits. We will also increase our monitoring of our planned and actual investments. Accessing our statements We publish our financial statements in PDFs. We want our PDFs to be accessible to everyone but we know that some older PDFs are not accessible to screen reader software. If you need a PDF in a different format, please contact us and we'll get back to you as soon as possible. View our financial statements: Financial Statements 2024-25 Financial Statements 2023-24 Financial Statements 2022-23 Financial Statements 2021-22 Financial Statements 2020-21 Financial Statements 2019-20 Financial Statements 2018-19 Financial Statements 2017-18 Financial Statements 2016-17 Financial Statements 2015-16 Corporate governance Find out more about our corporate governance, what shapes the way we work and learn how our organisation is governed. Read more Organisational structure Learn more about the structure of the University, from the faculties and schools to the teams behind-the-scenes in our professional services departments. title: University of Portsmouth | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> This Freedom of Information (FOI) publication scheme highlights all information that is publicly available about the University. The information is arranged under 7 headings to help you find what you want quickly: Who we are and what we do What we spend and how we spend it What our priorities are and how we're doing How we make decisions Our policies and procedures Lists and registers The services we offer We have separate publication schemes for our subsidiary companies – University of Portsmouth Enterprise Limited (UPEL), University of Portsmouth Investments Limited (UPIL), University of Portsmouth Services Limited (UPSL), and ASTA Technology Limited (ASTA). If the information you want isn't available in our publication schemes, you can make a Freedom of Information request. 1. Who we are and what we do This class has information on how the University is set up, including its legal status, location and contact details. It also includes information about organisations we work with. Close all 1.1 Legal framework This includes information about the legal and corporate status of the University. The legal framework for the University of Portsmouth Higher Education Corporation The University of Portsmouth Higher Education Corporation is the legal name for the University, but we're more widely known as the University of Portsmouth. The Education Reform Act 1988 and the Further and Higher Education Act 1992 set out how universities must be constituted. The Instruments and Articles of Government of the University The Instruments of Government (PDF) sets out the constitution for the Board of Governors, its officers and their roles The Articles of Government (PDF) sets out the rules for the conduct of the University, the procedures for the Board of Governors and Academic Council, and the appointment of senior officer

title: Board of Governors | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Board of Governors Find out more about our Board of Governors and their recent meetings Responsibilities of our Board of Governors This page sets out the role and responsibilities of our Board of Governors. You can also visit our who's who page to find out more about the individuals that sit on our board. If you need more detailed information, please contact Corporate Governance on corporate-governance@port.ac.uk Why do we have a Board of Governors? The University of Portsmouth gained its university status in July 1992. With this new status came the requirement that the University must operate in accordance with its Articles of Government and Instrument of Government, as approved by the Privy Council. The Board of Governors has ultimate responsibility for all University activities and for ensuring the efficient management of those activities. The following documents outline how the Board of Governors is governed, their primary responsibilities, and the Standing Orders of the Board and its Committees: Statement of Primary Responsibilities Articles of Government Instrument of Government Standing Orders of the Board of Governors and its Committees Scheme of Delegation What does the Board of Governors do? The Board of Governors is collectively responsible for determining the learning environment and mission of the University and for the direction of its activities. This includes a responsibility for determining the ongoing strategic plan of the University and for ensuring that the institution is being managed effectively. The Board of Governors discharges its responsibilities in accordance with the Committee of University Chairs (CUC) Code of Governance, which sets out its responsibilities for strategic planning, finance, audit, estate management, charitable status and public business. The CUC Higher Education C

title: Recruitment, Marketing and Communications
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Recruitment, Marketing and Communications > Internal communications Recruitment, Marketing and Communications The following student communications guidance for colleagues outlines general principles, channel options and responsible teams/departments for student communications. Principles University-wide communications Faculty-level communications Course and module-level communications 1. Principles As part of our commitment to fostering a student-centric academic environment, the University is adopting Compassionate Communication practices including committing to: a culture of kindness mindful communications timely communications inclusivity reflection and continuous improvement Our communications to students should also adopt: adopt our brand guidelines including an A-Z Editorial Style e.g. the date format should be 14 January and not 14th January align with the University’s values of being ambitious, responsible and open comply with data protection legislation in the use of student personal data right audience, right time, and right channel When choosing a communication channel, it's important to consider the audience, the message, and the urgency of the information. This is often written in summary as the three Rs - right audience, right time, and right channel. 2. University-wide communications Responsible: Internal Communications Team in Recruitment, Marketing and Communications for the majority of student communications. Contact: studentnews@port.ac.uk Library and Information Services for systems maintenance/upgrade messages. Channels: Weekly Student Newsletter - used for the majority of communications relevant to a large number of students MyPort

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Occupational Health Service Human Resources Occupational Health Service Role of Occupational Health The Occupational Health Service is available to provide confidential advice and support on any health issues. A Referral from Line Management will be necessary. Issues for which Occupational Health can be contacted include: Health assessments for all new staff, with follow-up advice as appropriate. Ergonomic advice/workstation assessment where there are health problems. Health assessment/medical for any staff driving on University business (only required for minibuses or vans) Advice regarding counselling Health surveillance for designated workers. Health Promotion Support for first aiders Advice for those travelling abroad on University business - via the Occupational Health website or in person if vaccinations are required. Arrangement of early eyesight test if required for health reasons - via referral from line manager. Advice on rehabilitation following sickness absence, regardless of how short term, if an employee is in need of support or advice or management are concerned. We also work with the Health and Safety team to help identify hazards in the working environment, advise on aspects relating to Health and Safety legislation and follow up all work related accidents. Occupational Health should always be contacted: Immediately following an accident at work involving an incident with sharps or contamination with body fluids via the University Sharps Hotline by calling 011 8207 6190 or by emailing help@cordellhealth.co.uk. Where there is a health problem, which may have been caused by or exacerbated by work or that is affecting wor

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Occupational Health Service Human Resources Occupational Health Service Referral Process Employees may be asked to attend Occupational Health at the request of their manager or HR. This may be for some of the following reasons: Concern that health is affecting work or work is affecting health. In pregnancy, following Risk Assessment Regarding long-term sickness absence Repeated short-term sickness absence whether or not there is an underlying health condition These referrals are done for the benefit of staff welfare and will entail an assessment either with the Occupational Health Physician, or an Occupational Health Nurse. For further support on the referral process, select an appropriate resource below: Introduction to Management Referrals - For Managers (video guide) Making a Referral to Occupational Health - Manager's Guide - Managers Guide What to Expect from a Referral to Occupational Health - Employee Guide Pre-placement Health Assessments Guide - Employee Guide What to expect from Health Surveillance Guide - Employee Guide Occupational Health Portal Access to Portal To access the Occupational Health Portal, Managers will first need to attend the ‘Training Awareness for the Occupational Health Portal for Management Referrals’. Please note: If you are a manager and have an urgent referral, and have not yet completed the above training, please request portal access by email to: mediwork@cordellhealth.co.uk Portal User Guides for Managers Please see the following short portal user guides to accessing and using the MediWork Portal: Mediwork Authentication Guide MediWork Managers Guide Mediwork Status and Meaning guide Migrating to M

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Occupational Health Service Human Resources Sickness Absence The Managing Sickness Absence and Ill Heath Policy sets out the procedure employees and their managers are required to follow in the event of absence from work due to sickness and the procedure to follow when ill health/disability prevents effective performance of an employee’s duties. It also identifies sources of support and guidance for employees and managers as well as outlining its use with other University procedures. A return to work discussion should be conducted after each occasion of absence, and in an appropriate confidential place. Whilst these meetings may be very informal the discussions should be recorded in note format, shared with both parties and kept locally in the employee’s personal file. Where an employee has been absent for long periods the return to work meeting should be held before the proposed return datewhich will assist in their re-introduction to the workplace. Managers are expected to complete the Return to Work Form after every period of sickness absence an employee has. The Return to Work form is available to Managers in iTrent. Guidance on how to complete this form can be found on Moodle. Occupational Health Referral To refer a member of your team to Occupational Health, you will need to complete a Referral form. Please also ensure a Risk Assessment for New & Expectant Mothers form is completed as soon as you are aware that your staff member is pregnant. There may be physical, biological and chemical agents, working processes and conditions that they could be at risk from and changes may need to be made to their work in order to ensure their health and safety. The form is to be retained by the relevant department and not sent to Occupational Health unless there are any problems which may need our help to resolve. In this case please Refer the individual to us and enclose a copy of the assessment. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Occupational Health Service Human Resources Mental Health If you are struggling with your mental health, you are encouraged to discuss this with your line manager and request a referral to the Occupational Health Service. If you are feeling overwhelmed by your symptoms and need more urgent support please contact the University Employee Assistance Programme on 0800 023 9324 (free from a UK landline) to talk to a trained counsellor, The Samaritans Freephone 116 123 (UK), or Mind. How to be mentally healthy at work is a booklet written by Mind, the mental health charity, and may be a useful resource. Wellness Action Plans - information for managers "This guide from the mental health charity Mind, is designed to be a helpful starting point in your journey as a line manager towards supporting your team members with their mental health at work. You might be: managing someone who is experiencing a mental health problem and want to know more about how you can support them interested in using the WAP with staff who are currently well, as a proactive tool to promote and maintain their mental health at work an HR professional looking to promote this guide to managers, to help them support the mental wellbeing of their teams" Mindful Employer The University has chosen to sign up to the Mindful Employer Charter, an initiative which is aimed at increasing awareness of mental health at work and providing support for businesses in recruiting and retaining staff. This demonstrates the University of Portsmouth's commitment to Dignity, Diversity and Equality by ensuring that as an institution it is open and accessible to all, and values its staff and stu

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Occupational Health Service Human Resources A job functional assessment is essentially a comparison of the requirements of a job with the capability of an employee to fulfil that role. This will have been recommended by Occupational Health (OH) where there is reduced capability for medical reasons, with or without adjustments to that role already being in place. There are various factors which may inform how the assessment is conducted: Nature of role Location of role Working pattern etc There is no 'one size fits all' approach to these assessments. Usually it is helpful to use the job description as a guide to ascertain the various tasks that comprise a job role and then measure to what degree that task is able to be completed by the employee. This can be done by observing, for example, how easy is it for a Caretaker to walk up and down the stairs of a building when they may be suffering with reduced mobility or by a discussion with a Lecturer about what percentage of their teaching load can be carried out where they might be affected by feelings of stress. The information can then be fed back to OH by management and may help us advise on future appropriate support, adjustments or management of the employee. It may be helpful for managers to talk through how to conduct a particular job functional assessment with OH. Please ring ext 3187 to speak to the OH Nurse for your area if necessary. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Occupational Health Service Human Resources Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement As you can’t always be sure whether someone has a disability or not, it is best practice to make reasonable adjustments for anyone who is having problems at work. This way you will have done all you can to try to help someone work to the best of their ability and may mean you recruit and keep the most talented people within the University. Talk to the employee to find out exactly what they are having problems doing so you have all the facts. Sometimes the person will be able to suggest a solution themselves, but don’t expect every disabled person or someone suffering with a medical condition to be an expert either on their own disability or on the adjustments that can be made. Remember you can ask for help from either the HR Business Partnering Team or Occupational Health (via a referral). It is best practice that adjustments you agree to support as a manager are documented and reviewed to ensure that they are effective. The Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement Record provides you as the manager and the staff member/student with a framework to guide discussions and help you keep a record of agreed adjustments, impact and review. It is simple to use and contains helpful notes and examples of reasonable adjustments. Managers should take further advice from the HR Business Partnering Team if they are unable to accommodate any adjustments suggested or advised by Occupational Health, Access to Work or other similar agencies. title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Staff Networks Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Disability Staff Network Co-Chairs: Cathy Banks, Emma Hart and Nic BonneSponsor: Paul MouldContact the Co-Chairs: disabilitystaffnetwork@port.ac.ukJoin the Network About the Network The Disability Staff Network is open to all colleagues from across the University. The network is co-chaired by Cathy Banks, Emma Hart and Nic Bonne. Membership is open to any staff member with or without a disability and is a safe space offering invaluable peer-to-peer support, help and advice. The network aims to: provide a voice within the University of Portsmouth for disability equality connect members of staff who have a disability or long-term health condition, and their allies, in a welcoming and supportive community advocate for awareness of disability related issues across the University, challenging perceptions around both visible and hidden disabilities. Joining the network By joining the network, staff will become part of a community with the opportunity to make a real difference. You’ll benefit from peer-to-peer support in a safe space and be signposted to internal and external support and advice. Join the Network. title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Staff Networks Equity, Diversity & Inclusion LGBTQ+ Staff Network Co-Chairs: Kathryn Thompson, Taomé Rees-Dottin, and Katie LovesonSponsor: Mike JohnsonContact the Co-Chairs: lgbtqstaffnetwork@port.ac.ukJoin the Network About the Network The LGBTQ+ Staff Network is an inclusive, supportive and welcoming community open to, and for, all LGBTQIA+ members of staff at the University. Co-Chaired by Kathryn Thompson, Taomé Rees-Dottin, and Bhavin Dedhia, the LGBTQ+ Staff Network comprises over 100 staff members from both professional and academic services. Our activities: support personal and professional development provide social and professional networking opportunities and events engage with the wider community. We provide a safe and non-judgemental space to discuss the experiences and challenges of being part of the LGBTQ+ family, as well as promoting and celebrating our diversity and working towards improving awareness and education for those that wish to learn more. We advise on training and university policies that link to or impact LGBTQ+ identities and campaign to ensure that our voices are heard, recognised and respected within the workplace. We work with the University to ensure members of our community are being treated fairly and without discrimination. We are members of the University’s Equality Diversity and Inclusion Consultative Group and Stonewall’s Diversity Champions scheme, and have a voice on the Professional Support Staff Association. We collaborate with Portsmouth Hospitals NHS Trust LGBTQ+ and Portsmouth City Council’s EDI groups, working to improve the local community and we have a presence at Portsmou

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Staff Networks Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Multicultural Staff Network Chair: Bhavin DedhiaSponsor: Dominic PageContact the Co-Chairs: multiculturalstaffnetwork@port.ac.ukFind out more about the Multicultural Staff NetworkJoin the NetworkVisit the Multicultural Staff Network's website About the Network The Multicultural Staff Network includes over 100 colleagues from across the University. The network is an invaluable space for peer support. It is open to people of all heritage, particularly those of Black, Asian, and minoritised ethnic and/or religious backgrounds. We provide a brave and safe space to discuss the experiences and challenges of being a minoritised person We advocate for issues relating to race, religion and cultural heritage We advise on University policies that impact on our members We celebrate cultural differences and improve cultural awarenessWe support the University in addressing the patterns of racial inequity; microaggressions and; lack of parity within promotions and development opportunities We have a voice on the Athena Swan and Race and Equality Charter SAT, as well as the Equality Diversity and Inclusion Consultative Group Two of our current areas of focus are supporting anonymous recruitment, which has increased the number of Black, Asian and minoritised ethnic colleagues being shortlisted, as well as advocating for effective action on bullying and harassment. Joining the network Make a difference by joining an impactful and diverse community of colleagues where you can celebrate and share what makes you you. title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Staff Networks Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Neurodiverse Staff Network Co-Chairs: Stephanie Campbell and Kirsten FarrellSponsor: Sherria HoskinsContact the Co-Chairs: neurodiversestaffnetwork@port.ac.ukJoin the Network About the Network The Neurodiverse Staff Network is an inclusive, supportive and welcoming community open to, and for, all members of staff at the University who wish to support and engage in learning and understanding of neurodiversity, including neurodivergent staff and allies. No disclosures or diagnoses are required to join the network. The network is co-chaired by Stephanie Campbell and Kirsten Farrell, with Sherria Hoskins as our UEB sponsor. Neurodiversity refers to the different ways the brain can work and interpret information. Some people refer to themselves as Neurodivergent (a term coined by Kassiane Asasumasu) meaning “neurologically divergent from typical,” which includes “Autistic people. ADHD people. People with learning disabilities. Epileptic people. People with mental illnesses. People with MS or Parkinsons or apraxia or cerebral palsy or dyspraxia or no specific diagnosis”. Some neurodivergent people identify as disabled, others do not. Those who do not have a neurological difference are sometimes described as neurotypical. Both neurodivergent and neurotypical people are part of neurodiversity. The network aims to: offer staff a place to share and support each other based on our shared experiences provide a space for collaboration to effect positive changes to attitudes and environments that affect our ability to participate fully in University life (and personal daily life) opportuniti

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Staff Networks Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Parents and Carers Staff Network Co-Chairs: Emma Scott-Ward, Emily Parry and Marion MoffattSponsor: Anne MurphyContact the Co-Chairs: parentandcarerstaffnetwork@port.ac.ukJoin the Network About the Network The Parents and Carers Staff Network is an inclusive community for everyone with parenting and caring responsibilities, and anyone with an interest in these areas. Co-Chaired by Emma Scott-Ward and Emily Parry, with Anne Murphy as our UEB sponsor, the network offers guidance, activities, and support, plus a forum to share information and events, and to provide feedback to the University on things that affect us. The network aims to: Share information and provide informal support and advice Encourage mutual support and networking Lobby the University to fulfil its aspirations to provide a flexible, family friendly working environment Raise awareness at all levels of issues facing staff with parenting and caring responsibilities by influencing university consultations, action plans and policies that impact on parents and carers. Joining the network Please fill out this form, or email parentandcarersstaffnetwork@port.ac.uk, and we’ll add you to the chat space in Google. body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Staff Networks Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Women's Staff Network Chair: Anna Vaernes and Kelly JackSponsor: Fiona HnatowContact the Co-Chairs: womensstaffnetwork@port.ac.ukJoin the Network About the Network The network aims to represent, inspire, support, and empower all those who identify as women at the University. It is member-led and welcomes all staff who identify as women or non-binary to join. We support, represent and advocate for women members across the University, both within Academic and Professional Services. We are planning to host three to four events annually, including networking sessions, guest lectures, and other events as suggested by our members. We also have the opportunity to take part in relevant policy updates and be included in various committees within the University. Joining the network If you are interested in joining the Network, please fill in this Google form or alternatively, email us at womensstaffnetwork@port.ac.uk. title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > What the Team Does Equity, Diversity & Inclusion What do the Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion Team do? We provide expert consultancy, enabling colleagues to play their part in creating a working, learning, cultural and social community where all colleagues and students feel they belong, are able to reach their full potential, and are treated with dignity and respect. Engagement and Raising Awareness Support our Staff Networks Publish a monthly EDI bulletin Organise events to mark cultural, religious and awareness events Informing Policies and Practices Guide department/faculties in developing and implementing EDI objectives and actions Support Athena Swan and Race Equality Charter submissions Training and Learning Identify University-wide and local EDI training needs Advise on commissioning trainers Deliver training including Intro to Equality Impact Assessments Data Collection, Monitoring and Evaluation Publish annual staff and student EDI reports Identify EDI trends How can you get involved in EDI at the University? You can help shape the University's culture and community in many ways. For example, you could: Join any of our staff networks Subscribe to our monthly EDI Bulletin Get involved in your department/school EDI committee Attend our Let's Talk... EDI discussion series Learn how to carry out Equality Impact Assessments and why they're important Share your ideas for advancing and celebrating equity at the University with us Work with our PGM Ambassadors to eliminate the ethnicity awarding gap For more information and resources, go to port.ac.uk/edi Explore Staff NetworksWhat the Team DoesEDI in ActionInclusion Calend

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > EDI News Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Equity, Diversity and Inclusion Newsletter Why not sign up for our monthly Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Bulletin to receive a monthly dose of EDI knowledge and a list of important dates for various cultures, religions, and EDI-relevant events? You will also find a list of our previous publications below for you to peruse at your leisure. Each of these has a brief list of the topics covered. 2025 March We are now moving to a bulletin every two months, meaning this article contains even more than before! Women's history, race in the workplace, endometriosis, LGBTQ+ history, striving towards equality in STEMM, a Stonewall highlight, and some urgent information about events happening this week! January Starting the year off strong, the EDI bulletin covers a plethora of topics. Learn about various Lunar New Years, discover what it means to be an anti-racist for Martin Luther King Jr. (MLK) Day, engage in the significance of World Braille Day, an intro to the lead PGM Ambassador, a vital reflection for Holocaust Memorial Day and a quick recap of the importance of disclosing staff demographic information. 2024 December As the year comes to an end, we have filled this month's bulletin with some festive information and more Disability History Month articles, including the last 'Friday Five' tips, talk about Wicca and some of its misconceptions, explore the concept of digital blackface, and lastly, a light read on fun Christmas traditions from around the world. November This edition of the EDI bulletin aims to highlight Disability History Month (DHM), sharing a timeline of their civil rights

title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Equality Impact Assessment (EIA) Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Equality Impact Assessments (EIAs) Inequality, lack of diversity and exclusive cultures usually have many contributory factors, and therefore there is no single solution to fixing them. We also know that many of the challenges we need to tackle can be subtle forms of discrimination which may not be conscious or deliberate, but can culminate in significantly unequal outcomes.Creating a consciously inclusive environment requires everyone to contribute through their own behaviours but also through their work.The University aims to conduct an Equality Impact Assessment on every new policy, process, function or system, as well as when any changes/updates or reviews are made. When conducting an Equality Impact Assessment, policy owners should consider not only the impact on protected characteristics but also any potential implications for academic freedom and freedom of speech. This is particularly important for policies that affect teaching, research, curriculum design, and other academic activities. The assessment should consider whether the policy might inadvertently restrict legitimate academic discourse, debate, or inquiry, and if so, how this can be mitigated while still advancing equality objectives. Policy owners should consult with academic subject matter experts when necessary to understand disciplinary norms and contexts.An EIA is a way of checking that a policy does not directly or indirectly have a negative impact on any staff and students based on their age, disability, ethnicity/ race, gender, religion/ belief or sexual orientation.We also want to pro

title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > The Report and Support Tool Equity, Diversity & Inclusion The Report and Support Tool The University of Portsmouth is committed to creating a culture where everyone can study, work, and live, in a safe and inclusive environment of mutual dignity and respect. All staff, students, and visitors can report discrimination, misconduct, harassment or assault, or any form of unacceptable behaviour using this tool. You can report anonymously or get support from an advisor-the choice is yours. You can use this tool to report incidents of physical assault, sexual harassment, sexual assault, online harassment, hate crimes, stalking, bullying, or discrimination. This might be based on: race, ethnicity, nationality and/or culture religion and/or belief sex or gender/gender identity sexual orientation disability including learning disabilities, mental health, physical disabilities age This list is not exhaustive. You can use the tool to report something that has happened to you, something you have witnessed or been told about. Make a report The tool also brings together guidance/support and resource to help you decide what you might want to do or who you might want to talk to about your experience or situation. body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Reports Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Reports The public sector duty of the Equality Act 2010 requires that public sector organisations publish equality information and gender pay gap reports annually. This type of information is collected across the University in various forms and the aim of this page it to pull together relevant reports into one place. The information can then be used to help staff, students and the public access our monitoring data and other reports that provide insight into aspects of equality and diversity. This information will be used internally to assist in the completion of Equality Analysis and help support other aspects of the University's equality, diversity and inclusion work, such as equality objective setting and the Equality Action Planning. The latest Gender Pay Gap Report can be found here. title: Staff Essentials
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Staff Essentials Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. Home Staff Services Departments Resources The Academic Staff Association (ASA) aims to promote and foster a spirit of unity and cooperation among the academic staff of the University. It provides a means by which the academic staff may express its opinions and wishes to the Vice-Chancellor. The Association communicates on behalf of academic staff with the Vice-Chancellor and with any organisation on ASA matters through the Executive Committee. The Executive Committee consists of officers of the ASA and an academic staff representative from each department. The ASA is always looking for new staff members to help participate in the association. Any academic staff member is welcome to contact the Chair, Dr David Sanders on ext. 2565 or via email (david.sanders@port.ac.uk) for an informal discussion. © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) Supporting the mental health and wellbeing of all our colleagues is incredibly important to us, and our new Employee Assistance Programme will help us do just that. Whether you’re facing problems at work or at home, you can access impartial, independent and entirely confidential advice from qualified counsellors for any issues you may be facing, such as Anxiety; Bereavement; Stress; Depression; Workplace Issues; Trauma; Relationships; Finances and debt; and Family Difficulties. Accessing the Helpline Confidential 24/7 Helpline: The telephone helpline is available 24/7, 365 days a year and is completely confidential and to anyone needing mental health support. To access the helpline, simply call 0800 023 9324 from any standard UK landline or mobile phone Digital Resources and Self-Help:A wide offering of resources is also available and includes downloadable self-help workbooks, debt advice and more. Visit the EAP Platform to find out more: www.port.ac.uk/myeap Is it confidential? The University does not know who uses the service unless the individual personally chooses to tell someone about their contact with Vivup. Vivup do provide statistics to the University to show how many employees use the service and the broad types of issues raised, for example; ‘relationship breakdown at home’ or ‘bullying and harassment in the workplace. No information is ever passed on which could potentially identify an individual. title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > The Report and Support Tool Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Harassment Adviser Network The University is firmly opposed to any form of victimisation, bullyingor harassment involving any of its staff or students. Bullying or intimidation is offensive behaviour that humiliates or undermines an individual or a group. Such behaviour can be vindictive, cruel or malicious and may be repetitive. It includes: • unwanted conduct that has the purpose or effect of violating a person’s dignity, or creating an intimidating, hostile, degrading, humiliating or offensive environment for a person that is related to age, disability, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, sex or sexual orientation; • unwanted conduct of a sexual nature (sexual harassment); • treating a person less favourably than another person because they have been the subject of, or have reacted to, sexual harassment or harassment related to sex or gender reassignment. A network of harassment advisers is available to help all staff and students who feel they may have been bullied, or have themselves been accused of bullying. While the advisers can't directly advise you what to do, they can inform you of your options and offer support. The decision on what to do is always yours. If you believe you are the victim of behaviour of this kind please visit the following advice page where you can find details of how to contact our harassment advisers and other support networks. All discussions with a harassment adviser are confidential, and brief records of their discussions will be recorded purely for statistical purposes. This information is anonymous and will be held in

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pay, Reward and Benefits Human Resources Travel to work by bus and coach Travelling by bus is a sustainable and alternative way to travel around our busy city, so why not give the bus a go and take a break from the day-to-day bustle of driving. City bus services Portsmouth has excellent city bus services. You can catch local and intercity buses from the main bus station at The Hard Interchange or the city centre. The Hard bus interchange is a short 16-minute walk to the centre of the campus and the city centre is even closer. From here a large number of other services can be caught. Bus stops closest to the University campus include those on: Winston Churchill Avenue close to University House Cambridge Road by the Students’ Union Queen Street near the Northern Campus Check timetables and plan your route with Stagecoach or First Bus. You can also find live bus information on MyJourneyPortsmouth. 40% First Bus discount Get 40% off your monthly travel with First Bus payable by Direct Debit for your Portsmouth or Hampshire wide travel. Simply set up your Commuter Travel Club account via the First Bus website using your work email. Your discounted price is £36 per month for Portsmouth wide travel and £48 for Hampshire. Register on the First Bus webpage Select your employer from the dropdown menu – visible at the bottom of the webpage Use your company email address to validate access to the scheme First Bus will then email you a link to complete the online registration and purchase a subscription ticket. University Bus Service The University bus service runs term-time only on a circular route from Monday to Friday, close to where many staff

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pay, Reward and Benefits Human Resources Travel to work by ferry or hovercraft As an island city, we are well connected across the Solent to the Isle of Wight, Gosport and Hayling Island. There are a number of choices for foot and bike passengers and cars to the Isle of Wight. Gosport Ferry discount Travelling between Gosport and Portsmouth is faster by water, with the Gosport Ferry taking only 4 minutes to cross the harbour. Ferries run from 5.30am to midnight, up to every 7.5 minutes at peak times. The Gosport ferry terminal is by the bus station. On the Portsmouth side, the ferry terminal is located by Portsmouth Harbour Station and The Hard. From here it is a 16 minute walk to University House or 7 minute walk to Richmond Building. You can also take your bicycle on board, for an extra charge, and cycle to University House in just 5 minutes. At the time of writing, Gosport Ferry offers all staff a 10% discount on quarterly season tickets, with and without a bicycle. This reduces the cost respectively to £214 and £262 (prices correct as of the 02/04/25). To purchase your ticket you need to take the following to the Gosport Ferry ticket office: Original Staff ID card as proof of ID A photocopy of Staff ID card 2 passport style photos of yourself Download and complete the Gosport Ferry University of Portsmouth discount form Ferries to the Isle of Wight Wightlink offers a variety of season and multi-trip tickets which may help you to save if you frequently use the ferries. Full details can be found on their website. Portsmouth Harbour to Ryde Pier Head (Fast Cat Passenger Service). Journey time approx. 22 minutes. Portsmouth Gunwharf to

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pay, Reward and Benefits Human Resources Travel to work by Park and Ride The PR1 service runs every quarter of an hour during the week and every 10 minutes at weekends, connecting the Park and Ride with the Hard Exchange with the nearest stop to the University on Queen Street. Portsmouth Park and Ride costs an average of £2 per day, based upon purchasing a Commuterpass 50 (for up to five people travelling together). Paper tickets can be bought from ticket machines or a smartcard can be purchased and topped up online or from the smartcard machine in the Park and Ride building. Tickets cannot be purchased on the bus, they must be bought before boarding. For ticket information, route details and the timetables visit the Park and Ride website. You can read a book or newspaper, use the free Wi-Fi where available, and take your bike to make your onward journey quicker. The closest station to the University are Portsmouth and Southsea and Portsmouth Harbour. These stations are just a short walk or cycle from the University: Portsmouth and Southsea – 8 minutes walking and 5 minutes cycling Portsmouth Harbour – 15 minutes walking and 5 minutes cycling To plan your journey and view timetables visit the National Rail website. You can also find live train information on MyJourneyPortsmouth. Train discount Get 15% off travelling by train with an easitPORTSMOUTH card on Southern Trains and South Western Railway. Whether you live to the north of the city and travel in from Cosham or from outside of the city, the University is well connected. The easit card costs £5.90 and is available to University staff as part of the easitPORTSMOUTH network. Register for a card or login to your account on the easit website. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pay, Reward and Benefits Human Resources Travel to work by car Reduce road congestion and your petrol and parking costs by car sharing, using the Park and Ride service, finding a car-sharing buddy through EasitShare, or using other alternatives to travelling by car. However, we know some staff may need to drive and the University has a number of car parks with spaces available on a first serve basis with a University car parking permit. There are also a number of city car park discounts available to staff. Car share with colleagues Please check and follow the latest government advice on car sharing during the Coronavirus outbreak. You can use easitShare to connect with others looking to share their car journeys (and other commuting trips). Sign up for free on their website and you can immediately begin your search for a car-share match. Once you've found a match, the agreement can be flexible and fit around you. University car parking permits All staff (including part-time, hourly paid and casual staff) living more than 2 miles from the University can request a car parking permit. Staff living within the exclusion zone who are blue badge holders or who meet our other needs based criteria can also request a car parking permit. Please note this does not include PhD or Masters students working part-time in conjunction with their courses of study. To request a permit or to review your existing permit, please visit the UoP car parking permit website. The University has partnered with Parking Eye to manage our car parks. Car parking enforcement is through Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) . Once you have obtained your permit you must
