title: Clearing | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Clearing 2026 UCAS Clearing 2026 opens 2 July. What is Clearing? This video is hosted on YouTube. To show it to you, we need your okay to use their cookies – it’s how the video player works. You need to accept functional cookies for the video to play. You can update your choices using the button below, or anytime on our Cookie Policy page. Update cookie preferences What is Clearing? Clearing gives you another opportunity to find the best course for you - whether you've changed your mind, your results surprised you, or you're just starting to think about going to university. Clearing at Portsmouth could be the perfect option. View transcript Nana I'm really glad that I applied for Clearing. It definitely paid off. Cristina Clearing is a process of applying to university after the deadline for applying for your uni. So if you decide to change your course or to change your university, Clearing is how you can get into those universities. Jess I had originally applied to Portsmouth to do Sociology with Criminology, and then decided the course probably wasn't for me. So very, very last minute I decided in the summer that I wanted to switch courses and I found International Development and then it was too late to change through the normal application process, so I decided, well, the only option was to go through Clearing. Nana It was very nerve-wracking. I was afraid that I'd end up with nothing. However, that definitely wasn't the case and there was so much support and information online that was given to me. So it was quite straightforward. It wasn't difficult or scary at all. Jess It was surprisingly super, super easy. I was put in touch with my course leader and it was all sorted within the hour, basically, after results. Senura The first thing I would say to somebody applying through Clearing is, don't panic.

title: Research and innovation | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Our global research Responsible Innovation: solving problems, enriching lives, creating lasting impact We want to help shape the future and know it takes more than words to make a difference. That's exactly what our research aims to do. We're making a significant difference in the world. 77% of our research impact was rated as world-leading or internationally excellent in REF 2021, and we are working on preparations for our REF 2029 submission. Our belief in knowledge-sharing makes our research exceptional and relevant. Our approach is always collaborative. We work with local and global partners in education and industry. Our media ready experts also regularly contribute their knowledge and expertise to print, broadcast and digital media coverage of the issues their research addresses. View our Research and Innovation Strategy to find out how we're delivering globally significant research excellence, and explore below how you could become part of our research community with our PhD and research degree opportunities. Our research Research centres and institutes Our research centres, institutes and hubs bring together expertise and resources from across the University of Portsmouth to tackle pressing global issues. Discover with us Research areas Working with industry and academic partners, and across our 5 faculties, our researchers are leading their fields and creating impact. Discover more Areas of expertise Discover our unique, collaborative and multi-disciplinary areas of expertise. Discover more Our global research impact Our research responds to challenges, influences policy and helps change the world for the better. Discover the impact of our research Our research in the media Research blogs Read some of our blogs and case studies here, and get a snapshot of the broad scope of work we're producing. Rea

title: About us | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> We're a new breed of university Find out how we make a positive difference to the world and how you can join us Welcome to the University of Portsmouth We’re proud to be a breath of fresh air in the academic world – a place where everyone gets the support they need to achieve their best. We’re always discovering. We don’t fit the mould, we break it. Just as the world keeps moving, so do we. We’re closely involved with our local community and we take our ideas into the global marketplace. We partner with business, industry and government to set the course for a better future. And we educate and transform the lives of our students to help them achieve their potential. We stand out, not just in the UK but in the world. We excel in research areas from cosmology and astrophysics to cyber security, forensics and sport science. Since the first day we opened our doors, we've looked towards the future. And we're here to help you shape it. Our story Our Strategy Our facilities Our ambition Structure and governance Key dates Working at Portsmouth Contact us Our London campus Study in London at the University of Portsmouth, in the heart of Waltham Forest. Our purpose-built, capital city campus puts world-class teaching and industry connections on your doorstep, allowing you to fuel your ambition in one of the world's greatest financial centres. Discover more You may also be interested in Policies and standards Freedom of Information Publications Our staff Maps and directions | We use cookiesWe may use cookies to analyse visitor data, improve our website, show personalised content and give you a great website experience. For more information about cookies on our site and to tailor the cookies we collect, select ‘Manage settings’.Accept all cookiesDeny optional cookiesManage settings

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety The Health, Safety and Compliance Team provides help, advice and support to effectively manage health and safety across all departments and activities including teaching, research, staff and students. Our Policy This Policy has been produced for all staff and others to be aware of both the University's and their own responsibilities under the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act. Our Health and Safety Policy Our Health and Safety Commitment Key Information Report an Incident Reporting incidents, accidents, near misses and hazards is essential to enable us to improve the University's health and safety management. Report a Building Issue Reporting a gas leak, electrical / heating failure, or a water leak / flood on university premises. Health and Safety A-Z If you are looking for information or advice relating to a specific health and safety subject, please find more information here. Health and Safety Law Poster Health and Safety at work is governed by the Health and Safety Executive (HSE). To help you better understand your rights and responsibilities for staying safe at work, we encourage all employees to read the HSE Law Poster above. Contact Us The Health, Safety and Compliance Team provide advice and guidance relating to all health and safety matters within the University. Explore TrainingHealth and Safety TeamA to Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Department for Curriculum and Quality Enhancement
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Department for Curriculum and Quality Enhancement Department for Curriculum and Quality Enhancement Welcome to DCQE DCQE's mission is to enhance the quality of the student learning experience and promote student success. We do this directly through our student facing services and indirectly through our support for staff. Click on the quicklinks below for further information on the services that DCQE provide ... Quicklinks ‌‌ ‌‌ ‌‌ ‌‌ ‌‌ ‌‌ ‌‌ Explore DCQE Director DCQE Sections Tel Tales Blog Site UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Academic Registry
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Academic Registry Academic Registry About Us and Who to Contact Welcome to the Academic Registry! The Academic Registry plays a central role in our institution’s commitment to maintaining academic excellence. Our role is to develop and support an academic quality regulatory landscape which supports the highest standards of academic quality, through academic regulations and policies that support a fair, transparent, and effective learning environment. Our Mission Our Mission is to ensure the University’s academic quality environment complies with mandatory regulatory requirements, upholds academic standards, enhances the student experience, and supports all students, regardless of their background, to succeed. Our Values Academic Excellence: Promoting the highest standards of education and quality of student experience Integrity: Ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability in all our actions. Collaboration: Partnering with students, staff and external stakeholders to uphold and improve academic quality. Responsiveness: Adapting to the changing landscape of higher education through innovation and foresight. Our Objectives Working collaboratively across the University, and informed by sector leading-practice we will: Ensure all academic policies, regulations and procedures meet mandatory regulatory requirements whilst being efficient and maintaining a forward-thinking approach. Provide an assessment regulatory framework which supports student achievement, and eliminates unwarranted barriers to learning and success; Create a student and staff-centric academic quality environment that promotes high standards and continuous improvement; Generate and a

title: Board of Governors
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Board of Governors Board of Governors The pages on this website set out to explain what the Board of Governors is, what it does, and to make available a range of related information. Information has been summarised as simply and clearly as possible. If you need more detailed information, then please contact the Director of Corporate Governance, Adrian Parry on e-mail adrian.parry@port.ac.uk Any comments or questions about the site would be welcome! body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Academic Registry Academic Registry About Us and Who to Contact Welcome to the Academic Registry! The Academic Registry plays a central role in our institution’s commitment to maintaining academic excellence. Our role is to develop and support an academic quality regulatory landscape which supports the highest standards of academic quality, through academic regulations and policies that support a fair, transparent, and effective learning environment. Our Mission Our Mission is to ensure the University’s academic quality environment complies with mandatory regulatory requirements, upholds academic standards, enhances the student experience, and supports all students, regardless of their background, to succeed. Our Values Academic Excellence: Promoting the highest standards of education and quality of student experience Integrity: Ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability in all our actions. Collaboration: Partnering with students, staff and external stakeholders to uphold and improve academic quality. Responsiveness: Adapting to the changing landscape of higher education through innovation and foresight. Our Objectives Working collaboratively across the University, and informed by sector leading-practice we will: Ensure all academic policies, regulations and procedures meet mandatory regulatory requirements whilst being efficient and maintaining a forward-thinking approach. Provide an assessment regulatory framework which supports student achievement, and eliminates unwarranted barriers to learning and success; Create a student and staff-centric academic quality environment that promotes high standards and continuous improvement; Generate and a

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Rewards and Benefits Human Resources Pay and Recognition If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk Pay / Salary Information Please click here for information on pay scales; salary spine points; salary increases; cost of employment and the University's commitment to the Real Living Wage. Payroll Deadlines Information on Payroll Deadlines can be found here. Season Ticket Advances Please follow this link for eligibility details and guidance on how to apply for Season Ticket Advances. Cycle to Work Scheme Save money on your commute to work by purchasing a new bike and spreading the cost over a year through payroll deductions. This is a salary sacrifice scheme so you'll make savings by paying less tax and National Insurance contributions. The University's scheme is provided by CycleSolutions. Applications are open throughout the year, with a maximum spend of £1,500 (after discounts have been applied). Visit CycleSolutions for full information. Our Recognition Ecosystem Find out more about the Recognition Ecosystem at the University. Holiday Purchase Scheme The Holiday Purchase Scheme application window for 2025/26 is now closed. The Holiday Purchase Scheme allows eligible employees to apply for up to 5 days (37 hours) additional time off (pro-rata for part-time employees) in a leave year over and above existing entitlements to holidays, public holidays and University closure days. It is only open in August of each year. Relocation Expenses Provided certain conditions are met, the University will consider paying relocation expenses for newly appointed employees. Expenses are paid in line wi

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Rewards and Benefits Human Resources Leave and Absence Annual leave is an integral part of a staff members reward package and a legal requirement. Staff wellbeing is important and it is essential that managers work with their teams to ensure that annual leave is planned and taken on a regular basis for the entire leave year. For more information on annual leave, sickness absence and flexible working, please follow the links below. If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk Leave The University provides a generous leave entitlement and additional Christmas closure days. Full details can be found in the Annual Leave policy. Holiday Purchase Scheme The Holiday Purchase Scheme allows eligible employees to apply for up to 5 days (37 hours) additional time off (pro-rata for part-time employees) in a leave year over and above existing entitlements to holidays, public holidays and University closure days. It will open between July and August each year, to take effect in the leave year commencing in the following October. We will advise when the window opens via Staff News. Absence The University is committed to the fair and consistent treatment of staff in relation to sickness absence and to supporting individuals during periods of ill health. Find out more. Family Friendly Leave There are circumstances where members of staff have a statutory entitlement to or can request additional leave. Many of these instances relate to support for families. This includes Adoption Leave, Maternity Leave, Paternity Leave, Shared Parental Leave, Parental leave, Premature Birth and Neonatal Care Leave, Stillbi

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pensions Human Resources Local Government Pension Scheme (LGPS) (available to University of Portsmouth employees in Support, Administrative, Professional Services roles who started prior to August 2024, and all non-teaching Research roles) The Local Government Pension Scheme (LGPS) is one of the largest public sector pension schemes. It is one of the best schemes available in the UK and one of the biggest benefits for being a part of the University of Portsmouth (UoP) and we are proud to offer it to eligible employees. At UoP, the scheme is administered on our behalf by Hampshire Pension Services. It is a Career Average ‘Defined Benefit’ scheme, meaning the benefits you get are guaranteed, based on your earnings, and set out by regulations rather than being dependent on investment risks and financial markets You need to know about your pension, what it is worth to you, and what it means to be a member – this guide aims to point you in the right direction throughout your entire pensions timeline, from choosing Portsmouth to life in retirement… your LGPS TIMELINE - what you need to know about LGPS HOW YOU JOIN ESSENTIAL ACTIONS WHEN YOU JOIN WHAT DO YOU GET AND WHAT DOES IT COST? HOW DOES IT WORK? TRANSITIONAL PROTECTION & ‘McCLOUD’ OPTING IN & OUT DURING YOUR MEMBERSHIP ADDING TO YOUR PENSION LEAVING THE UNIVERSITY RETIREMENT Want to Know More? Hampshire Pension Services LGPS homepage (the official scheme webpage for your pension) Contact LGPS Free 'Understanding LGPS' webinars from Wesleyan Financial Services UoP Discretions Policy - any employer 'discretions' for LGPS that UoP itself makes a decision on are detailed in this document)

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pensions Human Resources Teachers' Pension Scheme (TPS) Teachers’ Pensions require a personal email address for their records upon being enrolled into the pension scheme. This is because your Teachers’ Pensions account stays unique to you throughout your working life regardless of your employer. The University will provide Teachers’ Pensions with the personal email address that has been provided on your recruitment application upon your appointment. You can update your personal email address on your My Pension Online account on the Teachers’ Pensions web site. My Pension Online Contribution rates Joining or leaving the scheme Printable Member Guides for New Starters, Active Teachers, Family Benefits and more If you are considering retirement, click for more information. Buy Extra Pension Ill Health Retirement Transitional Protection (the 'McCloud' Remedy) You can find information on types of retirement, how to get an estimate of your pension benefits and how to apply for your pension on our Retirement - Teachers' Pension Scheme page. You can find more information on the Teachers' Pension Scheme website. Retirement - Teachers’ Pension Scheme If you are considering retirement and are, or have been a member of the Teachers' Pension Scheme, you should discuss your retirement options with your Line Manager and Dean or Head of Professional Service. Please see the information below which you may find helpful. Types of retirement I want to retire on or after my normal pension age. What are my options? Information about Age Retirement I am considering retiring before my normal pension age. What are my options? Information about Early Retirement

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pensions Human Resources MyPensionPlus - Your Pension and Benefits Scheme Eligible members of the University of Portsmouth Academic Services Limited (UASL at Portsmouth) may join our ‘MyPensionPlus’ benefits scheme. This scheme comprises two elements – a workplace pension, and a choice of additional benefits to select from. Please note these pages are not relevant to colleagues based at the London Campus - please see Aviva for London Campus instead. Eligible employees are those who are salaried (not paid via fees or timesheets) and on a permanent/indefinite or fixed-term basis. MyPensionPlus uses Aviva, one of the UK’s largest pension providers, to provide their Workplace Pension to all members. We recognise just how important a good pension is, and are proud to offer a very generous employer contribution of 12%, with options to increase this further. This goes hand-in-hand with a Death in Service payment of three times your salary, and a choice of a flexible benefits that can be picked each January at no cost to yourself. This is one of the biggest benefits you have working at the university and whether retirement is in the distant future or just around the corner, the decisions you make now are equally important and that means you need to know more about what it is, what it is worth to you, and what is means to be a member. These pages aim to point you in the right directions throughout your entire pensions timeline, from choosing University of Portsmouth, to life in retirement… your MyPensionPlus Timeline - What you need to know BECOMING A MEMBER HOW IT WORKS: CONTRIBUTIONS HOW IT WORKS: BUILDING YOUR BENEFITS AFTER YOU JOIN DURING

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pensions Human Resources Your UASL Nest Pension UASL Nest is the pension for employees of University of Portsmouth Academic Services Limited (UASL) in casual, variable hours and hourly paid contracts, and all other employees and workers who are not eligible for any alternative scheme. If you aren't certain if this applies to you, your contract will confirm the pension your are eligible for. What is Nest? Nest is a workplace pension scheme for all, set up by the government and administered by a public body according to the interests of its members. It is a 'defined contribution' (also known as 'money purchase') pension scheme where you and the University contribute a percentage of your salary towards a personal 'pension pot', where it will be invested and has a chance to grow over time. Find Out More UASL Nest Timeline - our policy and guidance on your scheme, covering contribution rates and the benefits of membership Nest Homepage – the scheme webpage to learn more and access to your pension Member Portal & Guidance to use it – update your personal details and beneficiaries, manage your contributions and apply to retire Additional Contributions The Nest scheme offers flexibility in making additional contributions through a one-off payment made directly to the scheme, or by setting up a regular direct debit from your bank. Alternatively, you may request to increase the rate of contributions you pay each month on your salary, by emailing pensionenquiries@port.ac.uk with the details. body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pensions Human Resources NHS Pension Scheme In limited circumstances some employees are eligible to participate in the NHS Pension Scheme. You can find more information on the areas below by accessing the NHS Pension Scheme website. Upon enrolment into the NHS Pension scheme, the University are required to complete a joiner form detailing your home address and work email address. You can update your home address and personal email address via your NHS Member Portal. The NHS Pension scheme state on their website that if they cannot contact you, they may use the services of a third party to check the address shown on your credit history record to see if the address they hold is more up to date. This will leave a 'soft trace' on your credit record and will be visible to you if you access your credit report. This does not affect your credit rating. Total Reward Statement Contribution rates How to opt out NHS pension and absences Adding to your Pension Additional Pension You may increase your annual pension by purchasing an Additional Pension, either by lump sum payment or by regular contributions deducted from your University earnings. Follow the above link for further information, and instructions on how to obtain a quote and apply. Money Purchase Additional Voluntary Contributions (MPAVCs) Alternatively, you can build up a separate pension through NHS Money Purchase Additional Voluntary Contributions. This allows you to build up a flexible pension pot with either Standard Life or Prudential that is linked but separate to your NHS scheme. You’ll make separate contributions from your monthly earnings, the amount you contribute is invested

Aviva are one of the UK’s largest pension providers and we have established our London Campus scheme with them to provide you one of the best ways you can invest in your own future. We recognise just how important it is to have a leading company pension, and are proud to offer a generous employer contribution of 10% for you to invest and build upon. This is one of the biggest parts of your reward and benefits at UASL and whether retirement is in the distant future or just around the corner, the decisions you make now are equally important that means you need to know more about what it is, what it is worth to you, and what is means to be a member. Nest is a workplace pension scheme for all, set up by the government and administered by a public body according to the interests of its members. Nest Homepage – the place to start for information and access to your pension Member Portal & Guidance – update your personal details and beneficiaries, manage your contributions and apply to retire Additional Contributions The Nest scheme offers flexibility in making additional contributions through a one-off payment made directly to the scheme, or by setting up a regular direct debit from your bank. Alternatively, you may request to increase the rate of contributions you pay each month on your salary, by emailing pensionenquiries@port.ac.uk with the details. Nest Pension & Absences Family Leave (Maternity, Paternity, Shared Parental and Adoption Leave) During paid family leave you will pay contributions on the actual earnings you receive. If you move to reduced pay or statutory-only pay, the University will contribute based on the ‘normal’ pay you had prior to going on reduced pay. If you move to unpaid leave, neither you nor the University will make further payments until you return to work. However, if you move to unpaid leave for any reason while still in ‘Ordinary’ family leave periods (normally the first 26 weeks), such as by not being entitled to statutory pay, the University will continue making contributions while you don’t have to. Your funds will continue to be invested (including during unpaid periods) so may increase or f

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Pensions Human Resources Pensions News & Updates Changes to member contributions in Teachers’ Pensions and Local Government Pension Scheme from April 2026 From 1 April 2026, the salary bands used to calculate member contributions in the Teachers’ Pension Scheme (TPS) and Local Government Pension Scheme (LGPS) will be updated. These bands are reviewed each year and adjusted in line with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) to reflect changes in earnings and inflation. The contribution percentages themselves are not changing, but the salary thresholds will increase. If your salary remains within the same band, your contribution rate will stay the same. Any changes will be applied automatically through payroll from April, so no action is required from you. LGPS 2026/27 Member Contributions TPS 2026/27 Member Contributions If you are making monthly Additional Pension contributions in TPS, the amount may also change from April. Our Payroll team is currently awaiting the updated figures and will apply any adjustments once this information is received. If you would like more information about how this may affect you, please contact the Pensions Team. Pensions Awareness Week September 15th is Pensions Awareness Week 2025, and there’s never been a better time to learn more about your scheme and how it works, check up on the value of pension you’ve been earning, and give a little thought to your future. Whatever scheme you are in – MyPensionPlus Aviva, Teachers’ Pension, LGPS, NHS, London Aviva or NEST – your pension is one of the biggest and best benefits we are proud to offer. Each scheme works differently, so be sure to find some time to learn more

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Managing Sickness Absence The Sickness Absence and Ill Health Policy sets out the requirements employees and their managers must follow in the event of absence from work due to sickness and where ill health or disability prevents effective performance of an employee’s duties. It also identifies sources of support and guidance for employees and managers, as well as outlining its use with other University procedures. If you have management responsibilities and need to refer a member of your team to Occupational Health, you will need to complete a management referral formin line with the Occupational Health referral process If you are a member of staff who has been referred to Occupational Health, you can download our Staff Guide to OH Referral and Advice. How do I report my sickness absence? Employee self-service and sickness absence FAQs. What do I do if I am ill? You are required to notify your line manager that you are unwell and unable to attend work as early as possible on the first day of your absence. You should try to give your line manager an estimation of the length of sickness absence if you think it may last longer than a day. How long can I be off for, before I need a fit note? You can self-certify for up to and including 7 calendar days. If you are ill for longer than this, you will need a fit note from your Doctor. What do I do once I receive a fit note? You need to provide your fit note to your line manager who will then send it to People Operations via the People Hub. The fit note details will then be added to the HR system. How do I record my self-certified sickness absence if I am off for more t

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Referral to Occupational Health (OH) Overview You may be referred to Occupational Health (OH) for advice where your health is affecting your fitness to carry out your role, or your role is adversely affecting your health. A referral may be appropriate when:- Your sickness absence is long–term (four weeks or more) Your sickness absence is short-term, intermittent and/or frequent (five days sickness absence in the last 12 month rolling period) You require help and support with health concerns at work Health surveillance for Health & Safety regulatory purposes as required for specific roles, e.g., working with hazardous materials etc. You have had an accident at work Further information is provided in the University Managing Sickness Absence Procedure. Who does this apply to? Any member of staff employed by the University can be referred to OH because the University has a duty of care to ensure that you are fit for work and that work conditions do not have an adverse effect on your health. You may not refer yourself to OH. Staff who are experiencing health-related conditions or concerns that may be impacting on their fitness for work, should bring this to the attention of their line manager who will be able to complete an OH referral to obtain further advice and guidance. In exceptional circumstances, you may be referred by Human Resources (HR). What do I need to consider? You will be referred for one of the reasons shown above and your manager will explain this to you. Your manager will complete a standard Occupational Health Referral Form giving the reason for the referral and the specific questions rela

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Entitlement to Sick Pay for Variable Hours Staff Overview It is important that policies for Variable Hours staff reflect fair and consistent Terms and Conditions. Specifically, Variable Hours are entitled to claim Occupational Sick Pay in line with their contract of employment. Who does this apply to? Variable Hours staff and their managers or supervisors. What do I need to consider? Variable Hours staff are entitled to claim Occupational Sick Pay in line with their contract of employment, as follows: Service Full Pay (calendar days) Half Pay (calendar days) During 1st year 31 61 (after completing 4 months’ service) During 2nd year 91 91 During 3rd year 122 122 During 4th & successive years 183 183 An individual’s entitlement is calculated by deducting their absences due to illness during the twelve months immediately preceding the first day of absence from the Occupational Sick Pay entitlement according to their length of service. For example, an individual who has been employed for two years and who has two periods of sickness absence, each of 12 days, during the previous 12 months will have 98 days full pay entitlement remaining (equal to 122 minus 2 lots of 12). Variable Hours staff can claim Occupational Sick Pay only for the hours they would have worked on the days for which they are absent. Statutory Sick Pay Variable Hours staff may also have an entitlement to Statutory Sick Pay (SSP) if they satisfy the conditions prescribed by the Department of Work and Pensions. To qualify for SSP, the Government’s Statutory Sick Pay eligibility criteria must be met. Individuals would only need to claim SSP if their O

The hallmark is based on the attributes that we wish our people to demonstrate, with the aim of our supporting our students to acquire the Hallmarks of a Portsmouth Graduate (Education Strategy 2016-2020). The characteristics of the Portsmouth Hallmark are that we will be knowledgeable, informed, self-aware, self-motivated and engaged. More specifically, our people will take pride in realising the aims of the University of Portsmouth, as appropriate to their role: Have an excellent understanding of their role and the part they play in ensuring that the University achieves its strategic aims; Have the ability to work independently as well as collaboratively, coming up with creative and better ways of doing things, embracing change and challenges as they arise; Be engaged, with a sense of professional responsibility linked to a culture of valuing equality, diversity, difference, respect and sustainability; Be effective team players, able to communicate clearly and effectively, supporting all members of the University community; Be able to work in a range of environments, responding positively to new situations by being aware, flexible, agile and realistic in their expectations; Be proactively engaged in and proud of working for the University, and striving for excellence in all they do in order to achieve the best outcomes for the University and its students. Be passionate and effective advocates in promoting the University to the wider community. (extract from the new HR People

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Estates and Campus Services > Soft Services (Campus Environment) Estates and Campus Services Using Vehicles on University Business Booking a Vehicle These can be booked via e-mail to transport.bookings@port.ac.uk Please Note: 48 hours notice is required. A follow up copy of the booking form is no longer required as confirmation. All bookings are processed via a web based system designed especially for the University by the contractual hire company. Prices can be obtained by contacting Transport Section or Jane Sheldrake If you wish to take a vehicle overseas, please let the Transport Section know in advance with at least three weeks notice. Overseas travel will only be arranged with the approval of the Head of Department. Please ring for further information about overseas travel. No minibuses to be taken overseas unless the driver can prove he has a PCV licence and has passed the medical and test required by the University. Insurance The University vehicles are covered by a fully comprehensive insurance policy with an excess of £250.00. Please contact the Transport Section for information. Accidents If you are involved in an accident while driving or acting as the person in charge of a vehicle, you are responsible for completing an Accident Report Form in respect of any accident to the vehicle or any person, property, or other vehicle. You should submit this form as soon as possible to the Transport Section. If subsequent to a motor accident, the Police prosecute you must inform the Insurance Officer immediately. body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Conditions of employment Human Resources Disclosure and Barring Service Overview The University is required to carry out criminal record checks with the UK Government’s Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) on individuals in specific circumstances dictated by UK legislation. The University’s Human Resources (HR) department is responsible for administering DBS checks for staff where they are required for employment purposes. The HR department also administers DBS checks for staff who need to apply for an NHS Research Passport. Who does this apply to? The University will make it clear if and when individual circumstances require a DBS check to be carried out. Should you be required to be referred to the DBS for a criminal records check, you will need to arrange a time to visit the HR department to complete the online application. HR will not be able to submit the check without sight of appropriate original documents as evidence of your identity. This information will be submitted to the DBS service, who make the necessary checks, and return the completed certificate directly to you. You must then be able to produce this certificate as evidence of the outcome of the referral. What do I need to consider? In order to prove your identity, you will need to provide original qualifying documents (not copies) to Human Resources. Without these, it will not be possible to proceed with your application. What do I need to do? The application for a DBS check for a staff member or a job applicant follows these steps: The University will identify a need for you to be referred to the DBS. As soon as the University informs you that you require a DBS check

This service should also be used to request rechargeable works. The Helpdesk is manned from 08.30 until 17.15 Mondays to Thursdays and 08.30 to 16.15 on Friday. Outside of these hours a Voicemail answering service operates directing callers to the Lodge on 3418 for emergency items. Alternatively you can leave a message which will be dealt with on the following working day. An email facility is also available, Helpdesk. How to report a fault or request repairs and maintenance Whatever method is used to report a fault etc, please ensure that as much information as possible is supplied to enable a more efficient service to be provided. Specific information that is required is listed below: Who you are and your phone number Where the problem/job is. Please specify building,floor and room/door number and/or estates reference number if known What, if any, access limitations exist What the nature of the problem is. Please provide as much clear information as you can Is the problem an emergency, urgent or routine (see definitions below as a guide) You will be provided with a job reference number that you should note down. You will not recieve written notification of your job request unless it was made by voice or email when you will be notified of the job reference number. body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Conditions of employment Human Resources Research Passports Overview A Research Passport is the mechanism for non-NHS staff (that is, higher education institution employees who have no contractual arrangement with the NHS) to obtain an Honorary Research Contract or Letter of Access when they propose to carry out research in the NHS. An Honorary Research Contract (HRC) applies to researchers who plan to undertake research which requires access to patients and identifiable patient data and has a direct bearing on the quality of patient care (for example provision of healthcare, personal care and guidance on well-being). By having an honorary research contract the researcher comes under the umbrella of NHS indemnity and is therefore covered by the NHS against potential claims for negligent harm. A Letter of Access (LOA) is required by employees who plan to undertake research which requires access to NHS premises or access to patients and patient data with no direct bearing on the quality of care (for example qualitative interviews, focus groups, on-site analysis of patient data). The Research Passport scheme provides: One set of checks on a researcher conducting research in the NHS. One standard form completed by the researcher and his/her employer, and validated by an NHS organisation. A completed Research Passport which is presented to all the relevant NHS organisations. Faster study start-up. A Research Passport may be project-specific or may be valid for a period of three years for a number of projects. Who does this apply to? If you are either a member of staff or research student at the University, are proposing to carry out researc

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Information for Staff about EUSS Pages last updated: 12 August 2021 -------------------------------------------------- Recruitment of Non UK/Irish Nationals The way we recruit non UK/Irish nationals has changed. On 1 January 2021 UKVI launched a new points based immigration system for workers coming to the UK. -------------------------------------------------- Overview This webpage has been established to provide information and support to staff who may be impacted by the changes to free movement between the UK and the EU/EEA. EU,EEA and Swiss citizens had until 30 June 2021 to apply to the EU Settlement Scheme to protect their rights, to continue to live and work in the UK, and access public services like the NHS. Successful applicants obtain settled status or pre-settled status, depending on how long they have lived in the UK. Evidence of this status is provided digitally through an online service. The Points Based Immigration System The new points based immigration system came into effect on 1 January 2021 and impacts both EEA and non EEA citizens coming to work in the UK on or after 1 January 2021. Anyone who did not obtain EUSS in time will need to obtain a visa status under the new system to continue living and working in the UK. UKVI will, in some cases consider late applications to the EUSS if there is a genuine reason behind the delay in applying. University Contact Details Email HR-UKVI-Compliance@port.ac.uk if there are any questions or queries related to the EUSS or the new immigration in relation to staff employed by the University. Before emailing please read the FAQs page which may cover your quer

Providing the right support and development opportunities are crucial to our success. ‌01 Consider your career, consider your development Professional development forms part of your annual Performance and Development Review (PDR). Depending on your role, skills and experience your career will vary. Your development can focus on a number of areas including: Developing in your current role Preparing for new responsibilities Increasing your potential Aspiring to work in new or different areas Hint - Read further information on career development. 02 Plan your development Professional development is your responsibility. The first step is about taking ownership. Deciding what you would like to achieve, then making it happen. Progressing your career - taking ownership Who can help? Your skills, your strengths, your experience Identifying your skills Hint - Our training and development plan will help identify development needs and support career discussions. 03 Explore university opportunities See more opportunities on the Learning Resource Centre webpage. Networking @UP ‌ Development Programmes ‌ Women's development Personal effectiveness ‌ Mentoring and coaching Leadership and Management Research and Innovation Professional Services Learning and Teaching 04 Access additional University resources Learning resource centre Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) Job vacancies Extra opportunities at UoP Employability support Employee journeys Hint - Contact the EAP for an hours career guidance each year. 05

title: Research and innovation | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Our global research Responsible Innovation: solving problems, enriching lives, creating lasting impact We want to help shape the future and know it takes more than words to make a difference. That's exactly what our research aims to do. We're making a significant difference in the world. 77% of our research impact was rated as world-leading or internationally excellent in REF 2021, and we are working on preparations for our REF 2029 submission. Our belief in knowledge-sharing makes our research exceptional and relevant. Our approach is always collaborative. We work with local and global partners in education and industry. Our media ready experts also regularly contribute their knowledge and expertise to print, broadcast and digital media coverage of the issues their research addresses. View our Research and Innovation Strategy to find out how we're delivering globally significant research excellence, and explore below how you could become part of our research community with our PhD and research degree opportunities. Our research Research centres and institutes Our research centres, institutes and hubs bring together expertise and resources from across the University of Portsmouth to tackle pressing global issues. Discover with us Research areas Working with industry and academic partners, and across our 5 faculties, our researchers are leading their fields and creating impact. Discover more Areas of expertise Discover our unique, collaborative and multi-disciplinary areas of expertise. Discover more Our global research impact Our research responds to challenges, influences policy and helps change the world for the better. Discover the impact of our research Our research in the media Research blogs Read some of our blogs and case studies here, and get a snapshot of the broad scope of work we're producing. Rea

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Learning and Development Human Resources Staff Mentoring Programme About the Programme The success of our vision, to become the UK’s top modern university and one of the top 100 universities in the world by 2030, relies upon the creativity and innovation of staff and the development of a culture where people feel passionate about their contribution to the University. Mentoring helps us realise the potential of our staff in achieving organisational and professional goals. It also supports the development of a culture of equality and diversity and contributes to our Athena SWAN and HR Excellence in Research award commitment. The University of Portsmouth’s Staff Mentoring Programme is an easy-to-use online platform designed to promote and develop meaningful mentoring partnerships. The scheme supports staff to engage in effective development and embody our drive for excellence. Staff Mentoring at UoP How do I join the Staff Mentoring Scheme? FAQs and case studies If you have any queries regarding the programme please email staffmentoring@port.ac.uk. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Learning and Development Human Resources Learning and Development Title Developing your Supervisory Skills (2 day programme) Target Audience This workshop is aimed at staff new to a supervisory role or aspiring to move into one. It is also relevant to more experienced supervisors wishing to refresh their approach. Outline These two, linked one-day workshops cover all the essential aspects of supervisory management and seek to develop practical skills and confidence. Within a confidential environment, participants can share and learn from each others experiences. The aims of the workshop are to: Outline for participants the basic principles of effective supervision and highlight the skills needed by supervisors in order to succeed Explore in more depth some of the critical areas of skills, ie self-management, team leadership, promoting and supporting team work and tackling poor performance Provide opportunities for participants to apply good practice principles to representative case studies and scenarios Create a supportive environment in which participants can recognise their strengths and identify how to build on these. Learning Outcomes At the end of the workshops participants will: Have explored practical strategies for dealing with challenging situations by undertaking individual and group exercises Have reflected on their role; assessed their current level of supervisory skill and identified ways in which to improve these. Pre-event Preparation Participants are asked to reflect in a structured way on their supervisory role and to attend the workshops prepared to share their thoughts with other participants. Cancellation/Failure to Attend Policy A cancellation fee of £25, chargeable to your Department, will apply should you cancel your attendance within less than 14 days of this event, or if you should fail to attend on the day. You will not be charged for cancellation if you are able to send a substitute where appropriate, or if you are unable to attend owing to sickness absence or compassionate leave. title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety The Health, Safety and Compliance Team provides help, advice and support to effectively manage health and safety across all departments and activities including teaching, research, staff and students. Our Policy This Policy has been produced for all staff and others to be aware of both the University's and their own responsibilities under the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act. Our Health and Safety Policy Our Health and Safety Commitment Key Information Report an Incident Reporting incidents, accidents, near misses and hazards is essential to enable us to improve the University's health and safety management. Report a Building Issue Reporting a gas leak, electrical / heating failure, or a water leak / flood on university premises. Health and Safety A-Z If you are looking for information or advice relating to a specific health and safety subject, please find more information here. Health and Safety Law Poster Health and Safety at work is governed by the Health and Safety Executive (HSE). To help you better understand your rights and responsibilities for staying safe at work, we encourage all employees to read the HSE Law Poster above. Contact Us The Health, Safety and Compliance Team provide advice and guidance relating to all health and safety matters within the University. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Support for staff with a disability The University of Portsmouth offers a range of support services for individuals with disabilities and their managers. Human Resources The HR Business Partnering teams provide professional guidance and support for individuals and managers across all HR policies and procedures across the University, with each team supporting different Faculties and Professional Services. They also provide advice on employment solutions and best practice / legislative advice. Occupational Health Advisers The University Occupational Health Service provides impartial advice to employees and managers following a clinical assessment of need. Employees may be formally referred by their line manager or HR. Advice will be provided on adjustments that are recommended in respect of the employee’s fitness to work. Equality and Diversity Office The Equality and Diversity (E&D) team is able to give advice, guidance and support, in addition to that available from other business areas, around disability and reasonable adjustments. Disabled Staff Forum The University has a Disabled Staff Network for consultation with disabled staff and for their mutual support. Please encourage disabled staff to participate in these sessions. The Disabled Staff Forum exists for all staff 'with a long term illness, disability or infirmity that affects their everyday life or the work that they do'. Employee Assistance Programme The University provides an Employee Assistance Programme for staff, which offers a free, confidential helpline (0800 023 9324) that provides unlimited access to information, advice and support, and onward

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Leaving My Role Human Resources Changing Roles within the University Managing the transition Leaving your current role If you are transferring between departments or changing roles within the University, it is likely that you will not require access to the same systems or folders in shared drives, or to certain business applications (e.g. Finance). Your Head of Department/Service or their agreed delegate should make arrangements to ensure that access to areas no longer required are withdrawn by Information Services and/or the appropriate Business System Owners and any equipment belonging to the department is returned prior to the date of transfer. Induction into your new role The University of Portsmouth values the importance of providing an effective and timely induction for all new members of staff to ensure they successfully integrate into the University, their department, their team, and to their role. You can find out more information in our Induction section. The line manager of your new role should provide you with an Induction checklist as well as a Training and Development plan. Secondments Taking up a secondment Following your induction into your seconded role, it is recommended that there are regular reviews with your new line manager to ensure you are supported, and also there is an opportunity to review performance. Day-to-day line management during the secondment will be the responsibility of your new line manager. Further information can be found in the University's Secondment Policy. Keeping in touch Your substantive line manager may wish to keep in touch during your secondment. In addition, any HR matters that may cont

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Leaving My Role Human Resources Leaving the University Resigning from my job If you are planning on leaving your role at the University, you are required to provide written notice of your intention to leave to your line manager, subject to the terms specified in your Contract of Employment or Letter of Appointment. The University standard notice periods for staff depend on the type of contract. Before submitting your written notice of leaving to your line manager, you will need to consider the following: Leaving the University- Issues to Consider. You will also need to agree with your line manager your last paid day of employment at the University. To prepare for leaving the University you should familiarise yourself with the Staff Leavers Procedures and Checklist. Fixed-term contract employees - Core Role If you are employed on a fixed term contract, HR will write to you as necessary before the end date of your contract and you will be given access to the redeployment module, which will enable you to apply for university vacancies before they are published to the wider audience. Managing my retirement If you are retiring from the University, you should bear in mind the guidance in Leaving the University- Issues to Consider. If you intend to return to the University as either a paid employee or visiting staff member, please ensure that HR is advised of the details by your line manager. If you are thinking about retirement and are a member or have previously been a member of one of the pension schemes offered by the University, our Pensions section provides links to the schemes’ websites and member portals where further information abou

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Effective Leadership and Management We will align our academic and professional service staffing capabilities with our University Strategy 2015-2020, investing in development opportunities for staff that enable them to contribute fully to the delivery of our vision. One of the ways which we will do this is by ensuring that leaders and managers are able to support, engage and direct their colleagues in their work, performance and development and that the workforce is aligned and equipped to meet the changing University needs. Our ability to deliver academic excellence; provide an outstanding student experience and create a workplace culture that is supportive and inclusive, will all be influenced significantly by the quality of leadership and management at all levels. Read more in the People Strategy (2016-2020), which builds on the vision and values described in the University Strategy. Leadership Attributes At the Senior Leadership Awayday in April 2016, Peter Brook, Director of HR launched a new set of Leadership Attributes. Informed by focus groups, facilitated by the Leadership Foundation for Higher Education and Learning & Development Advisers, the attributes are designed to provide a clear set of expectations for our leaders and also to allow staff to understand what to expect from leaders at the University of Portsmouth. The purpose of the attributes is to Guide and support current and aspiring managers and leaders. They define what good leadership looks like in our organisation and provide a clear set of expectations and behavioural indicators as a benchmark. Inform a new leadership development

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Planning ahead Identifying the competencies and numbers required to implement the team’s objectives and determining how to meet these requirements. Delivering the University Strategy To achieve the ambitious aims of our mission and vision, we have developed the University Strategy. The Strategy brings to life what we need to do to make our Vision a reality. Creating a new role Grading a new role* The grading process covers the evaluation of a "new" job/role. The definition of a 'new' job/role is where no other similar jobs/roles exists within the University at the grade required. Research Roles* The University HR Department supports academic staff involved in the recruitment and employment of Research Staff. Academic Roles* The UoP locally agreed academic role profiles* are generic descriptors of the demands and responsibilities required of most academic staff. For enquiries about grading and new posts, please contact hrgradings@port.ac.uk Cost of employment figures(COE) and salary scales You will find the current COE figures at the links below: Academic, Support & Research Staff * Part-Time Hourly Paid Staff (PTHP)* Please note that the cost of employment figures for Academic and non-Academic positions differ due to the employer's pension contribution rates. You can view the University of Portsmouth current Salary Scales listing all spine points and grades. Progression to the top of each grade is by annual increment. *Internal only Explore HR HomepageMy RoleLeaving My RoleManaging My TeamHR Business Partnering TeamLearning and DevelopmentHR SystemsMy Rewards and BenefitsPensionsPayrollOccupational Heal

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Recruitment and Selection and Visiting Academics (prevention of illegal working) Help and information with recruiting employees to fill vacant positions in the team or inviting visitors to the University. Recruitment and selection information Our main Recruitment and Selection webpage provides information and guidance on the recruitment of staff at the University. The Recruitment Process flowchart describes the whole recruitment and selection process and the steps you need to follow. Recruitment and Selection Training Recruitment and Selection training is delivered in a modular style, allowing staff involved in the interview process to access the required modules in a timely manner. Visas and immigration Our Visas and Immigration Quick Reference Guide provides information and guidance on these areas. Visiting Academics (prevention of illegal working) The University has a legal responsibility to prevent illegal migrant working in the UK under the Asylum and Nationality Act 2014. Our Visiting Academics pages provides information for those who wish to invite visiting academics and researchers, external visiting lecturers, external examiners and practice assessors to carry out activities (either paid or unpaid) for the University. You can find information for non- EEA applicants working at or visiting the University of Portsmouth Explore HR HomepageMy RoleLeaving My RoleManaging My TeamHR Business Partnering TeamLearning and DevelopmentHR SystemsMy Rewards and BenefitsPensionsPayrollOccupational Health ServiceEquality and DiversityContact usStaff Surveys HR A-Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Con

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Welcoming new team members Developing and implementing induction programmes and managing probation for new team members. Preparing for a new member of staff How to organise a computer account for a new member of staff (this will also allow you to request telephone options, Electronic Document Management (EDM) access and a University Staff (Campus) card. Only nominated members of staff are able to request staff accounts. Information and guidance on the University Staff card, including card information and replacing a lost card is available from the Information Services MyPort page. Managing induction Our Managing Induction page aims to support you in planning an effective induction programme for new members of your team for whom you have line-management responsibility. You will also find information on the Induction Checklist, a structured framework which includes a health and safety checklist, and a list of essential University policies that all staff should familiarise themselves with. Line managers should also complete a Training and Development plan for new staff and ensure that staff new to the University attend a Staff Induction Conference. Managing probation The Probation Policy is intended to support staff in a new role and to highlight training and development needs or measures required to help them fulfil their new responsibilities. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Developing my team Providing training and development opportunities for team members. The way we work Read about the University Mission and Vision , The Way We Work and The Portsmouth Hallmark Core training The University of Portsmouth expects managers and staff to complete certain Core Training - ideally during the probationary period. Some elements are provided through e-learning and others through workshops. The Personal Development Review (PDR) process and developing team members We all benefit from taking time out to think about how our work is going and what we are trying to achieve. The Personal Development Review (PDR) process offers a chance to discuss your own thoughts and hear another perspective on your contribution to the department and University. Professional, honest conversations enable us to celebrate successes and discuss problems. University of Portsmouth Staff Mentoring Scheme Mentoring is a supportive opportunity to aid staff in identifying and achieving career goals. It supports the development of a culture of equality and diversity and contributes to our Athena SWAN and HR Excellence in Research award commitment. The University Staff Mentoring Scheme is available for all staff. Exploring development opportunities for team members At the University of Portsmouth, we are committed to providing high quality learning and development events and opportunities, to ensure that staff understand their role. Our Learning and Development pages provide information about the full range of development opportunities and support for leaders and managers available at the University. The Learning an

title: Human Resources

Academic Workload planning Academic Workload Planning is about planning future workloads for the next academic year, providing a broad overview of all activities undertaken by each member of staff. Measuring and Monitoring Performance The Performance and Development Review (PDR)- (for all staff) provides an opportunity to discuss your own thoughts and hear another perspective on your contribution to the department and University. Professional, honest conversations enable us to celebrate successes and discuss problems and feedback provides us with information about what we are valued for and what we could improve on. Departments can plan better too, when they understand the unique skills and experience each person brings to their job. Managing Under-Performance Procedure (MUPP) The Managing Under-Performance Procedure sets out how the University will manage employees whose performance has fallen below the standards expected. The primary objective is to help employees to improve performance to an acceptable level in line with the requirements of their role, as summarised in the job description, through support and guidance. The procedure also outlines the steps and actions to be taken in the event of failure to improve performance. FAQs are available for the Managing Under-Performance Procedure. Disciplinary Procedure The Disciplinary Procedure document sets out the procedure employees and their managers are required to follow in the event of disciplinary issues that arise at work. It provides a clear and transparent fram

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Rewarding team members Recognition Ecosystem Our Recognition Ecosystem brings together a range of complementary schemes (including financial and non-financial awards) tailored to different contexts and levels of recognition, ensuring that contributions of all kinds are valued and celebrated. Find out more on our Recognition Ecosystem pages. Long Service Awards The University recognises staff who have completed 25 and 40 years continuous service with the University, with a letter from the Vice-Chancellor celebrating the long service, contribution and accomplishments of the recipient. Continuous service includes service with Portsmouth Polytechnic and members of staff who transferred from Portsmouth College of Art, Design and Media and The Solent School of Nursing, with no break in service.
