title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Supporting leavers Developing and implementing transition programmes for departing team members. Leaving my role Leaving my Role-Information for staff, to help during either changing roles or leaving the University. Notice periods The University notice periods* for staff depends on the type of contract. Staff Access to University Facilities and Leavers' Procedures - including returning resources Staff Access to University Facilities and Leavers' Procedures - This document sets out access levels, rights and responsibilities for staff and what should happen when a member of staff is leaving the University. Calculation of leave entitlement Leave entitlement for part-years on starting and leaving is calculated on complete calendar months of service. On leaving employment with the University, any outstanding leave entitlement to date of leaving should be taken. If this is not possible, outstanding leave entitlement will be paid with the final salary. Leave already taken that is in excess of entitlement will be deducted from the final salary. Support networks Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) including- Management Support Pensions information The University of Portsmouth offers good quality pension schemes to its employees. The Pensions section provides links to the schemes’ websites and member portals where further information about pensions can be found. General information about workplace pensions can be found on the UK Government website where you can also find information and advice about the State pension and pension entitlements. If you have any other queries, please email pensionenquiries@port.ac.uk

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Managing change Guiding your team through change, and embedding new practices into business operations. Organisational Change Policy: Redeployment and Redundancy (OCRR) The Organisational Change Policy: Redeployment and Redundancy (OCRR) sets out the University’s approach to dealing with organisational change that may lead to a reduction in employee numbers, however, the approach is applicable to any form of organisational change, in particular, the sections on consultation and communication and the business case approval process. Staff may be placed in redeployment for a variety of reasons, i.e. redundancy, end of fixed term contract, as a result of MSA, MUPP, Disciplinary process etc. The redeployment module has been created to allow all staff on the redeployment register direct access to all vacancies as HR receive them. Staff placed in redeployment will have visibility of new vacancies and the opportunity to apply for them, before they are published on the recruitment website. If you have a member of staff who is being placed in redeployment, the Online Redeployment Guide- Applying for Vacancies (Candidates Only) will provide them with help and guidance on applying for vacancies. Online Resources Change is an ever-present and essential part of daily life, and the University is a constantly evolving organisation. While this can make it a dynamic and vibrant place to work, change needs to be managed effectively if we are to realise the benefits within the University. Delivering Successful Change presents a four-stage approach to planning, delivering and embedding change. Whatever your role or grade, t

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Managing My Team Human Resources Managing leave and absence Annual leave The annual leave year for all staff runs from 1 October to 30 September in any year and you should ensure your staff take their entitlement within this time-frame. Leave entitlement Academic and Research staff receive an annual leave entitlement of 35 days (259 hours) per year. These staff include: Deans, Heads, Professors, Readers, Researchers, University Tutors and Graduate Teaching Assistants. Professional Services staff receive an annual leave entitlement of 32 days (236.8 hours) per year. Part-time staff have a pro-rata entitlement to leave based on the number of hours/weeks they work (their fte). Temporary staff on variable hours contracts have an entitlement to 32 days per annum pro rata and this will be calculated in proportion to the number of hours worked. Booking leave All annual leave must be submitted for your authorisation in advance, via Employee Self Service (ESS). Information on how to authorise annual leave is available on our HR Systems page. If you are going to be absent for an extended period, you should set up a redirect for a colleague to authorise leave requests in your absence. Information about this is also available on our HR Systems page. Carrying over leave By booking hours with Friday being a shorter working day for many staff, there will be occasions when odd hours of leave are created which cannot immediately be booked if less than half a day. When these accumulate to half a day, they can be booked, but there is a chance that at the end of each Scheme year there will be a few odd hours left over. A maximum of 41 hours annual leave o

Supporting team members on secondment The University of Portsmouth is committed to creating an equitable, developmental and motivating working environment, which values and empowers people at all levels. Secondment opportunities can be an appropriate means of achieving this commitment. The Secondment Policy provides the basis for all formal internal and external secondment arrangements across the University. International Secondment The term ‘international secondment’ refers to the temporary transfer of a member of staff from their normal role to do another role on a fixed term basis outside of the UK internationally. The University's International Secondment policy is designed to provide managers and staff with information about the main issues to be considered when seconding a member of staff overseas. Regrading an existing role The Regrading Process covers the evaluation of an existing job/role that is currently filled by a member of staff, where it is deemed the role has grown in level of responsibility/competence- find out more about regrading an existing role*. Dealing with flexible work requests The Employment Rights Act 1996 gives all employees the chance to request a change to their contractual terms and conditions of employment to work more flexibly. This could be a change in their working pattern, or perhaps the number of hours they work. For more information, read the Right to Request Flexible Working -Guidance Disclosure and Barring Service Checks (DBS) The University’s Human Res

Ill health, managing sickness absence and medical issues Our Managing Sickness Absence page sets out the process both employees and their managers are required to follow in the event of absence from work due to sickness and the procedure when ill health/disability prevents effective performance of an employee’s duties. You can also download the Managing Sickness Absence Procedure. The University’s Occupational Health Service provides advice to individuals and managers on work related health problems and health problems that can affect work. Our Occupational Health pages also contain information and advice on Mental Health. The Mindful Employer Charter is an initiative which the University of Portsmouth is signed up to, which works to increase awareness of mental health in the workplace. The University Disability Guidance sets out the process for putting in place appropriate adjustments for staff with disabilities. It is recognised that some disabled employees may need time off for a reason relating to their disability.The Disability Absence- Guidance for Managers and Staff provides clarity to both managers and staff. The University provides an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) which offers a free, confidential Helpline which provides access to information, advice and support (including management support), and onward referral to telephone or face-to-face counselling where appropriate. The service is designed to help individuals deal with a range o

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Contact us Human Resources The HR Business Partnering (HRBP) Team The HRBP Team brings together specialist functions across HR business partnering, employee relations, EDI and staff wellbeing. Strategic HR Business Partners: Work closely with Executive Deans and Directors to align people strategies with local goals and people plans. They act as a consultant and advisor on workforce planning, organisational design, culture, and change initiatives. They use data and insights to identify opportunities that improve performance, engagement, and capability, while also anticipating future workforce needs. They also play a key role in supporting and embedding wider HR initiatives, ensuring consistent adoption and impact across the institution. Employee Relations Team: Each Employee Relations Adviser (ERA) is dedicated to providing support to various departments and faculties and should be regarded as the initial point of contact for all employee relations matters including: Early intervention; tailored guidance on simple ER issues through to complex case management, including: performance management; absence management; discipline and grievance; cessation of fixed term contracts. The team is also heavily involved in grading reviews and the review of employment policy and best practice and will lead on trade union liaison on employment and policy matters. HR Coordinator: Involved in the whole range of HR activity to support the team and managers including progressing contractual work as well as more complex appeals preparations, Job evaluation (grading) Equity, Diversity and Inclusion Team: Provide expert consultancy to support the creation of

Please click on Employee Benefits Platform (Vivup) to access the portal. Overview The platform has 100’s of fantastic discounts and offers, you’re sure to find many that are relevant to you. Whether you’d like to save money on your grocery shopping, days out and entertainment or household purchases, there are plenty of offers for you. In addition to the benefits platform Vivup also provide our Employee Assistance Program and Your Care Wellbeing Platform. Who does this apply to? All current University employees are eligible to access the Employee Benefits Portal. Access to the portal is free while you remain an employee of the University. Please note that if you are on a Zero Hours contract or are not a direct employee of the University, you are not eligible for this benefit. What do I need to consider? As access to the portal is dependent on your being a University employee, if you are planning to leave the University then you will need to make sure that you don’t sign-up to discounts that will become available after you leave, as your account will be cancelled. This wouldn’t affect existing discount cards or evouchers with money still on them. The portal is only for University employees. You are welcome to purchase items through the portal for friends and family but you must not share your login or password or let anyone else use the portal with your account. What do I need to do? You will be automatically registered with the portal within one month of joining the University and will receive an email from email@employeebenefits.vivup.co.uk asking yo

Please click on the links below to find more information on the options available to you. If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk Employee Assistance Programme Speak in confidence to an independent and fully qualified counsellor 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year to discuss any emotional, personal or work-related problems. Talking to one of Vivup’s experienced counsellors or psychotherapists can help you understand a problem, make a tough decision, try a different approach or just learn how to cope with a difficult situation. The aim is to support you and find a solution to whatever is worrying you. Occupational Health The University's Occupational Health Service provides advice to managers and employees on work related health problems Your Care - Wellbeing Platform We believe we are all happier and healthier when able to take control of our own wellbeing, so in addition to our Employee Assistance Program you also have access to ‘Your Care’, a dynamic wellbeing platform offering a range of personal, emotional and financial wellbeing tools to help you be a happier, healthier you! Your Care includes: • Assessments - Identify the risk of developing leading lifestyle-related diseases, along with mental health and financial scores • Goals - The option to set health-boosting goals with small, sustainable lifestyle changes • Activity Data - Connectivity with your heal

A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. Ferries and Hovercraft As an island city, we are well connected across the Solent to the Isle of Wight, Gosport and Hayling Island. There are a number of choices for foot and bike passengers and cars to the Isle of Wight. Find out more. Trains and Buses Travelling by train or bus significantly reduces your environmental impact locally when compared to the equivalent car journey. The closest train stations to the University are Portsmouth and Southsea and Portsmouth Harbour and are a short walk to the University. In addition to the city bus services, there are regular daily direct coach services to and from Portsmouth and London, Birmingham, Heathrow and other locations around the country together with the University Bus service which runs during term time. Find out more. Cars and Bikes The university offers a salary sacrifice scheme to allow the purchase of electric and hybrid vehicles together with discounted car parks. Discounts are available on bikes including a salary sacrifice scheme to purchase a bike for travel to work. Find out more. Employee Benefits Platform (Vivup) The Employee Benefits Platform offers travel discounts across accommodation, holiday discounts, cruises, travel and airport extras and car hire transport. body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Rewards and Benefits Human Resources Financial Benefits and Wellbeing If you have any queries on the information in these pages, please contact reward@port.ac.uk Pension Schemes The University is proud to offer competitive and leading pension schemes to all our employees, and these form one of the biggest benefits available to you as an employee. Please go to our Pensions page to access the information you need to know. LGPS Shared Cost Additional Voluntary Contributions (AVCs) As a member of the Local Government Pension Scheme, you have a unique opportunity to invest in your financial future in a cost efficient way. Shared Cost AVCs allow you add additional value to your pension while saving National Insurance and Tax on these contributions. A basic rate taxpayer can add £100 to their pension contributions at a cost of only £68.12. AVCs allow you to add extra value to your pension at any stage of your career. They are linked to LGPS but work more like a personal pension where you build up a separate pot of benefits with additional flexibilities, such as choosing how much you wish to pay and how it is invested. The University have secured a partnership with mymoneymatters which allows you to save both National Insurance and Tax on all AVC payments. To find out more please visit the mymoneymatters website. We encourage you to read through the information and understand the options available. Wesleyan - Pension Webinars Wesleyan Financial Services (who are specialist financial advisors, regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA)), offer a free public webinar for any members of either the Local Government, Teachers' or National

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Rewards and Benefits Human Resources Personal Development If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk Please click on the links below to find more information on the personal development opportunities open to you as an employee. Learning Development (L&D) The Learning & Development team offer a wide-range of workshop events, eLearning courses and resources to support you in your staff development. Linkedin Learning Linkedin Learning is a great online learning and training resource, available free to all students and staff through your University account. For more information visit the Linkedin Learning page. Docebo The University’s new Learning Management System (LMS). Designed to offer an engaging user experience, Docebo enables colleagues to access eLearning, register for events, and explore a wide range of learning and development resources. Visit Docebo for Core and Role-specific training. Learning at Work Staff can access a range of courses from the Learning at Work department. You can choose from a range of qualifications including: Certificate in Higher Education in Professional Studies (Level 4) Bachelor's and Master's courses in Business, Engineering, Maritime Studies, Creative Technologies and Enterprise, Applied Computing, Applied Science and Professional Studies PGCert and MSc courses in Occupational Health & Safety Management and Occupational Health & Safety Management and Occupational Health, Safety & Environmental Management Enrolment is in August / September and January each year. As a member of staff, you may be entitled to a 30% discount on course fees. Staff Apprentic

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Rewards and Benefits Human Resources Life Insurance Some employees may be automatically enrolled in our workplace life insurance scheme at no cost. Whether an employee is in-scope for this depends on their contract, pension scheme entitlement and status (see 'Eligibility' below). Where eligible policy members die in employment while aged under their normal State Pension Age, their nominated beneficiaries may receive a life insurance payment equal to 3 times basic annual salary, pro-rated for part time. This cover is provided from day 1 of eligibility, and is not subject to medical assessment or exclusions based on pre-existing medical conditions. Eligibility To identify whether you are in scope for this policy, please consider whether you fall into one of the following scenarios: Are eligible for Local Government Pension Scheme (LGPS), Teachers’ Pension, or NHS Pension Scheme in your employment contract, but have opted out of that scheme for any reason Are an active contributing member of ‘MyPensionPlus’ Are a member of UoP London, whether actively contributing in a pension scheme or opted out If you are an active contributing member of LGPS, Teachers’ Pension or NHS Pension Scheme, then you will normally be eligible for a ‘Death in Service’ grant as part your ongoing membership. Further information will be found within individual scheme guidance. You should maintain up-to-date beneficiaries directly through your pension provider - this page is not relevant to you and you must not use the nomination process below as it will take no effect. Other employees who do not fall into one of the above categories are not enrolled in a life in

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Rewards and Benefits Human Resources Recognition at the University of Portsmouth People are the driving force behind everything we do and each success and accomplishment is a collective achievement, made possible only through the exceptional contributions of individuals and teams across our University. Recognising and celebrating these achievements in a manner that is fair, inclusive, consistent and transparent is essential to fostering an environment where everyone feels valued, supported, and inspired to do their best work. Recognition is not just about rewarding success, it’s about reinforcing the attitudes, actions, and values that will help us realise our ambitions and support a culture where we are all motivated to excel, collaborate, and innovate in pursuit of our strategic goals. The Recognition Ecosystem Our Recognition Ecosystem is a framework to promote a culture of appreciation and values-led behaviour across the University, embedding Appreciative Management Practice that reflects and reinforces who we are: Ambitious, Responsible, and Open. The Recognition Ecosystem brings together a range of complementary schemes tailored to different contexts and levels of recognition, ensuring that contributions of all kinds are valued and celebrated. Each of these is directly linked to our values, ensuing what we celebrate and reward reflects who we are and what we stand for as a university. By aligning with those values, we recognise the behaviours and mindsets that drive successes, not just the outcomes, and credit how achievement is reached when done so in ways consistent with our identity and aspirations. Our Values Ambitious Res

title: Maps and directions | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Getting here and getting around Plan your journey to and from Portsmouth, and explore our campus when you get here Already here? Find your way around our campus. Download our campus map Train Our closest station is Portsmouth and Southsea. It’s a 5-minute walk from the buildings on our Guildhall campus. There are daily direct trains from London Waterloo and London Victoria (2 hours), and regular services from Southampton, Bournemouth, Chichester and Brighton. We’re only a 2-hour journey away from Bristol too. You can plan your route with National Rail or Trainline. Car Take the M275 into Portsmouth (signed 'Portsmouth West') whether approaching from the east (A3(M)/A27) or the west (M27 Junction 12). Ignore the junctions signed to Southsea and Hilsea. Park and ride As with any city, parking in Portsmouth is limited. Therefore it's best to use the park-and-ride service at the entrance to the city on the M275. The cost is £4 per vehicle and buses run into Portsmouth every 12 minutes. Up to 5 people can travel using 1 ticket. Parking in the city If you want to park in the city, follow signs for 'Historic Waterfront/Seafront' from the end of the M275. This takes you across 3 roundabouts to traffic lights by Unicorn Gate (entrance to the naval dockyard) and St John's Cathedral. These are the traffic lights at the top of the Guildhall Campus map – the route you take from here depends which part of the campus you're heading to. Car parks and limited on-street parking is available in the city centre for a small charge, but spaces fill up quickly. You’ll need a visitor’s permit if you want to use a University car park. To apply for one, email car.parking@port.ac.uk. Sorry, we can’t guarantee you a space in advance. If you have an electric car, there are a number of charging points around the city that you can use. title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > A to Z Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Accident and Incident Reporting Introduction An Accident is an unplanned event that results in injury or ill-health. An incident may be a near miss or undesired cirumstances that have the potential to cause injury, ill-health damage or loss. The law requires that certain work-related incidents are reported to the local authority or the Health and Safety Executive. Near Miss: An event that has occurred without causing harm, but it had the potential to cause injury, ill health or damageUndesired Circumstance/Hazard: A set of conditions or circumstances and sources that have the potential to cause injury, ill health and damage. The investigation of near-misses and undesired circumstances are an important step in accident prevention. These terms does not include Dangerous Occurrences which are certain unintended, specified events which may not result in a reportable injury but do have the potential to cause significant harm. These are specifically identified in Schedule 2 of the Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations (RIDDOR) 2013, and is reportable to the enforcing authority. See the Accident Reporting Policy for details. Reporting All accident and incidents , however minor, should be recorded and reported to the Health & Safety Office by submitting a HS1 Form. These are now available on service manager/ hornbill via the Health and Safety pages. If you are unable to access please contact the Helath and safety office by emailing hsservicedesk@port.ac.uk. If there is any concern that health is affecting work or that work is affecting health as a result of any incident at work, please talk to your line manager about a referral to Occupational Health. More information on the

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Learning and Development Human Resources University Staff Apprenticeship Scheme In support of our training and development of staff, the University provides opportunities for existing staff to undertake apprenticeship programmes, subject to the approval and commitment of their line manager. Apprenticeships are funded by the Apprenticeship Levy and offer University staff the opportunity to work and be paid while learning and working towards completion of a recognised qualification. Intermediate and Advanced level apprenticeships can offer skills and knowledge to support you in your role, developing your existing skills and enhance your effectiveness. These apprenticeships are available in any sector or industry and the University HR Apprenticeship Team will source an appropriate training provider to deliver the training. Degree apprenticeships were launched by the Government in April 2015 and provide the opportunity for staff to gain a degree as part of their apprenticeship training. The University of Portsmouth currently offer a range of Degree Apprenticeship programmes including Master’s level, and plans are underway to increase our offering in future. Find out more Apprenticeships – What are they and how do they work? All apprenticeships are work-based and therefore require the support of your line manager. While working towards an apprenticeship, staff will be required to spend 20% of their working hours on off-the-job learning and line managers will need to facilitate this. Additionally, an appropriate mentor will need to be identified to support staff throughout the apprenticeship programme to ensure learning is effective. UoP Deg

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > My Role Human Resources Promotion to Associate Professor and Professor Overview This guidance provides an overview of the process for preparing and evaluating applications to Associate Professor and Professor, and providing feedback to applicants. The University awards the title of Associate Professor or Professor on the basis of exceptional performance and externally-recognised distinction. The process applies only to Associate Professor and Professor, and not to other grading activity under Higher Education Role Analysis (HERA). The award is made by the Vice-Chancellor who will take advice from Faculty and University Promotions Committees, internal and external assessors, and referees. Professors are banded according to the level at which they are working and their contribution to the University. Band 1 will normally be for both internal and external candidates promoted or appointed to Professors. Band 2 is the appropriate Band for Professors who are able to demonstrate academic standing and distinction that is significantly greater than that required for promotion or appointment to Band 1. Band 3 is for the small number of professors who are able to demonstrate and sustain an unusually highly accomplished track record of academic achievement and leadership. Both Associate Professor and Professors are expected to make a full contribution to all aspects of University activity. Applications for Professorial re-banding are biennial, the application round for re-banding will open again in autumn 2026. Who does this apply to? title: Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP)
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: Special > Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP) Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP) AHEP at the University of Portsmouth The Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP) aims to promote excellence in HE management and administration and to raise the profile of those roles in HE as a profession through the development of best practice and high standards in the area. Not only are they a representative voice for HE Professionals in the sector, they also provide support and guidance for members in developing your their careers. Find out more here. The AHEP core commitment for members are to: Empower your career journey Improving your professional practice Supporting your professional development Championing the vaules of: Credible, Collaborative, Inclusive and Personable They provide a Professional Framework linked to 8 key professional behaviours, which can be used whatever your role or level. Membership is open to everyone, whether you are and existing or aspiring HE Professional. Click here to find out more about why you should join, or here to explore the membership levels available. To find out more check out the National Events and local activities via our Padlet AUA@UoP Community. If you have any questions or would like to find out more, please contact the UoP Branch Advocate: Nikki Sowerby. Nikki is also the South Region Network Deputy Coordinator. title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > EDI in Action Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Athena Swan What is Athena Swan? The Athena Swan Charter is a national charter mark that recognises the advancement of gender equality in academia. It encompasses representation, progression, and success for all. Established in 2005, it set out to encourage and recognise commitment to advancing women's careers in science, technology, engineering, maths, and medicine (STEMM) employment in higher education and research. Expanded in 2015, it also covers gender equality in the arts, humanities, social sciences, business, and law (AHSSBL) disciplines. It also focuses on professional and support colleagues working in the higher education sector and on the issues facing all genders, including trans & gender non-conforming colleagues and students. Our Athena Swan Journey The University initially received an Athena Swan Bronze Award in November 2014. In April 2018, we retained it, recognising our commitment to gender equality. Lastly, we succeeded in receiving our Athena Swan Silver Award in 2022. Our submission was developed across the university at all levels, including academic, research, professional, and support colleagues. This award gives us just a few years to complete the vital work developed in the Athena Swan Silver Action Plan. If you have any questions about Athena Swan please contact the Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion Team (equality@port.ac.uk). Athena Swan Principles In supporting the Athena Swan Charter, the University of Portsmouth has signed up for the 8 key principles; we embed these principles within our policies, practices, action plans, and culture. We commit to: Ado

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > A to Z Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Driving The Driving on University Business Procedure is designed to secure a healthy and safe working environment for everyone who drives a motor vehicle as part of their employment or study and to ensure compliance with current legal requirements. It includes the use of vehicles owned, leased or hired by the University and vehicles owned by staff or students when used to conduct University business. Areas of responsibilities are defined and all staff should make themselves aware of their responsibilities. Managers are responsible for ensuring annual driver declaration - licence verifications are completed in accordance with the procedure. The process in Appendix 1 should be followed. The annual driver declaration - licence verification should be completed and in place where required through the Corporate Health and Safety self Service Portal within Service Manager. On approval and authorisation the driver will be placed on the Record of University Authorised Drivers. Estates and Campus Services will continue to process requests for hire vehicles through transport.bookings@port.ac.uk. Requests will only be processed if an annual driver declaration - licence verification is in place and in date. title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Recruitment, Screening and Hiring Human Resources Procedure for conferring the title of Emeritus Professor Introduction The award of Emeritus status is exceptional in nature and is conferred upon someone to recognise their sustained, distinguished service to the University and their field of study and their ability to continue on this trajectory as an Emeritus. The status is considered to be mutually beneficial and facilitates a continuing relationship, on a voluntary basis, between the retiree and their faculty. An Emeritus Professor is not required to exercise any teaching, research or supervisory role or any administrative, executive or representative function. The conferment of the title Emeritus Professor does not imply any continuing status as an employee or officer of the University, but there is an expectation that the Emeritus Professor will wish to continue a voluntary and unpaid association with the teaching and/or research activities of the University and that may include the title ‘Emeritus Professor of the University of Portsmouth’ in publications. Any award of the title of Emeritus Professor is made entirely at the discretion of the University. Criteria The title of Emeritus Professor may be awarded to a Professor who is leaving the University to retire and: who has made a substantial contribution to the reputation of the University; who has a sustained and distinguished service to their field of study; who has been in continuous employment with the University for at least five years before retirement ; With whom the University would like a continuing association by participating, on a voluntary basis, in activities of t

title: Human Resources
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Human Resources > Recruitment, Screening and Hiring Human Resources Visa brake for Skilled Workers The UK government is introducing a restriction that refuses visas for people applying for certain UK visas on specific routes and for specific nationalities. This is known as a visa brake. Taking effect on 26 March 2026. This visa brake will apply to Afghan nationals only, applying overseas and intending to apply for a Skilled Worker visa only. If an Afghan national is already in the UK, on a valid visa route, they will be able to apply for an in-country extension or move to another visa route if eligible. The visa brake is not permanent, and will be reviewed by the government regularly. Please refer the the FAQs for more information. If you have any questions or concerns please contact the Compliance Team via the People Hub. title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Biological Safety Training Leader Christina Scott, Biological Safety Officer Target audience: Pre-requisite for anyone with responsibilities in biological laboratories; particularly those working in CL2 laboratories. Aspirational for staff and students who wish to widen their knowledge as part of development. Aims This training will give participants an understanding of: - Why biological safety matters - How to contain biological agents - Risk assessment process relating to biological hazards Learning Outcomes As a result of attending, participants will be able to: - identify the legislation applicable to the safe use of biological agents - outline the requirements of containment level laboratories - outline the hazards associated with the biological agents they handle - outline the control measures that may be used. Explore Training Biological Safety Biosafety Awareness COSHH - Chemical Safety and COSHH Dealing with Chemical Spills and Emergency Preparedness Drone Training DSE Online training Fire Awareness First Aid Genetic Modification Safety Manual Handling Mental Health First Aid Pressure Systems Safety Risk Assessment Safe Use of Compressed Gas Working at Height Health and Safety TeamA to Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Biosafety Awareness Leader Christina Scott, Biological Safety Officer Target audience: New staff and post-graduate students working in biological laboratories or with potentially infectious material. Aims This training will give participants an understanding of: - Sources and routes of infection - Overview of containment levels - Control measures to prevent infection - Overview of risk assessment process Learning Outcomes As a result of attending, participants will be able to: - identify sources and routes of infection - work safely in laboratories Explore Training Biological Safety Biosafety Awareness COSHH - Chemical Safety and COSHH Dealing with Chemical Spills and Emergency Preparedness Drone Training DSE Online training Fire Awareness First Aid Genetic Modification Safety Manual Handling Mental Health First Aid Pressure Systems Safety Risk Assessment Safe Use of Compressed Gas Working at Height Health and Safety TeamA to Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety COSHH - Safe Use of Chemicals Target audience: Pre-requisite for Technicians who use chemicals in their work and aspirational for Technical Staff who wish to widen their knowledge as part of development. Aims This training will give participants an understanding of: - the legislative requirements for the use of chemicals and substances hazardous to health in the workplace. Learning Outcomes As a result of attending, participants will: - have awareness of the legislation that applies to the use of chemicals and substances hazardous to health - be able to outline the classifications of hazardous substances and associated ill-health risks - know what to include and where to find the relevant information for your COSHH risk assessments - be able to identify suitable and sufficient control measures and how to maintain them - understand when exposure monitoring and health surveillance is required - understand the requirements for emergency planning Explore Training Biological Safety Biosafety Awareness COSHH - Chemical Safety and COSHH Dealing with Chemical Spills and Emergency Preparedness Drone Training DSE Online training Fire Awareness First Aid Genetic Modification Safety Manual Handling Mental Health First Aid Pressure Systems Safety Risk Assessment Safe Use of Compressed Gas Working at Height Health and Safety TeamA to Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Dealing with Chemical Spills and Emergency Preparedness Training Target Audience: Pre-requisite for Technicians who work with chemicals. Aims This training will give participants an understanding of: - Impact of chemical spillages on the University - Overview of the relevant legislation - Risk assessment which includes identifying chemical hazards, routes of entry and people at risk - Procedure for dealing with chemical spillages - Control measures for chemical spillages - Spill prevention Learning Outcomes As a result of attending, participants will be able to: - Explain why dealing with spillages correctly is important and the consequences of not doing so - Identify the legislation relevant to spillages of hazardous materials. - Outline the hazards associated with chemicals that are used on campus. - Outline the procedures for dealing with spillages. Explore Training Biological Safety Biosafety Awareness COSHH - Chemical Safety and COSHH Dealing with Chemical Spills and Emergency Preparedness Drone Training DSE Online training Fire Awareness First Aid Genetic Modification Safety Manual Handling Mental Health First Aid Pressure Systems Safety Risk Assessment Safe Use of Compressed Gas Working at Height Health and Safety TeamA to Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Uncrewed Aerial Systems (UAS) Training Leader Martin Schaefer, UAS Safety Officer Toby Meredith, UAS Safety Officer Target Audience: - Staff and PhD students wishing to operate Uncrewed Aerial Systems on University business - Staff who manage staff likely to operate Uncrewed Aerial Systems on University business - Staff who are required to sign off risk assessments Aims: This course is a prerequisite to be allowed to operate Uncrewed Aerial Systems (UAS) on University business under the UAS Arrangement. It is designed to give participants the required knowledge to operate UAS within the scope defined by legislation and the UAS arrangement and to operate UAS safely. Please note that this does not constitute training as defined by the CAA, and additional external qualifications may be required depending on the Uncrewed Aerial Vehicle and environment of operation. Learning Outcomes As a result of attending, participants will be able to: - Identify different types of UAS and what they can be used for - Identify current UK laws and regulations on operating UAS - Identify where you can and can't fly a UAS - Outline the requirements to operate a UAS in the UK - Outline the University structures that manage UAS operations in the UK and abroad - Outline the University staff and student policies on operating UAS on University business - Outline record keeping requirements as set out in the policies - Outline UAS incident reporting - Plan a UAS operation in the Open Category Delivery This course is based on independent online learning (Moodle), including a quiz, and a 3 hour in person workshop. Completing the online component is a prerequisite for the workshop. Explore Training Biological Safety Biosafety Aw

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Display Screen Equipment Online Training and Risk Assessment The Health and Safety Office provide online Display Screen Equipment training and risk assessment. The objective is to provide computer users with training in accordance with Health and Safety legislation, Approved Codes of Practice and HSE guidelines. The target audience is all University of Portsmouth members of staff who are computer users. The duration to carry out the online training is approximately 20 minutes in total and a quiz then needs to be completed. The online DSE training can be accessed on Docebo, as part of your Core Training Learning Plan. This training needs to be completed every 3 years or sooner if your circumstances change. i.e: new computer equipment, new furniture (desk/chair), change in location/building, change in desk setup. If you have any queries or require any further information, please email hsservicedesk@port.ac.uk. Please also see this Occupational Health webpage containing health and wellbeing resources for staff working at home. title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Online Fire Training It is a legal requirement (Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005) that employers must provide appropriate information, instruction and training to employees, during their normal working hours, about the fire precautions and the fire equipment in the workplace. Confirmation of how the University responds to these specific requirements is shown below in our FAQ section. For access to the Online Fire Safety Training please follow this link to Docebo and select My Learning Plans. You will then be asked to enrol. This training comprises of a course and an assessment. The training will not be registered as complete unless both elements have been completed. For assistance with the Online Training please contact hsservicedesk@port.ac.uk. Frequently Asked Questions How often should the on-line Fire Safety training be completed? The on-line Fire Safety training should be refreshed every 3 years or sooner if your circumstances change. i.e: if you move buildings/area within the building. title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety First Aid Training Programme Training is organised through the Health and Safety Office, contact hsservicedesk@port.ac.uk. All first aid courses must be accredited by the HSE. To become a First Aider: 3 day First Aid at Work Training (On completion of this course, you will become a University First Aider and receive an allowance) To Re-certify: 2 day First Aid at Work Re-certification course taken after 3 years. To become an Emergency First Aider: 1 Day Emergency First Aid Training Refresher Training Workshops - Refresher training sessions are open to all First Aiders who wish to practice unused skills or discuss relevant First Aid topics. We advise First Aiders to attend at least one workshop within the 3 year period between courses as part of the Annual Refresher Training, but these are optional. A certificate will be issued on completion of the workshop. title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Target audience: - Members of staff who carry out manual handling tasks Aims This training will give participants an understanding of: - The Manual Handling Operations Regulations and other relevant Health & Safety legislation - The University’s Policy and Procedures in relation to manual handling - Risk Factors, Assessment and Control Measures Learning Outcomes On completion of this course, candidates should be able to: - understand the legislation covering manual handling operations at work - understand manual handling risk factors - appreciate the need for a risk assessment to be completed - comply with the control measures - be aware of the mechanical aids available - understand efficient movement principles Explore Training Biological Safety Biosafety Awareness COSHH - Chemical Safety and COSHH Dealing with Chemical Spills and Emergency Preparedness Drone Training DSE Online training Fire Awareness First Aid Genetic Modification Safety Manual Handling Mental Health First Aid Pressure Systems Safety Risk Assessment Safe Use of Compressed Gas Working at Height Health and Safety TeamA to Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Pressure Systems Safety Target Audience: Pre-requisite for Technicians who work with pressure systems Aspirational for Technical staff who wish to widen their knowledge as part of development Aims To provide candidates with an understanding in the following subject areas: - Overview of the Pressure Equipment Regulations 1999 & the Pressure Systems Safety Regulations 2000 - Safety valves - Types of failure in pressure systems - Inspection and maintenance of pressure systems Learning Outcomes - Outline the main requirements for relevant legislation - Outline the main features and safety accessories associated with pressure systems - Identify pressure systems that can be found within the University - Outline how the legislation applies to the purchasing of new and pre owned equipment - Outline procedures for operating, maintaining and testing of pressure systems and equipment - Outline the modes of failure for pressure systems and equipment - Outline methods to prevent failure Explore Training Biological Safety Biosafety Awareness COSHH - Chemical Safety and COSHH Dealing with Chemical Spills and Emergency Preparedness Drone Training DSE Online training Fire Awareness First Aid Genetic Modification Safety Manual Handling Mental Health First Aid Pressure Systems Safety Risk Assessment Safe Use of Compressed Gas Working at Height Health and Safety TeamA to Z UniversityofPortsmouth Home Students Staff Contact and departments Home Staff services Departments Resources © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Introduction Risk assessments are an important step in protecting the University and its employees, as well as complying with the law. The process involves identifying the risks in the workplace with the potential to cause real harm, to evaluate whether enough precautions have been taken to prevent this harm, or whether more needs to be done. Accidents and ill health can ruin lives and affect the University as well if output is lost, equipment is damaged, insurance costs increase, or the University is taken to court. It is a legal requirement to assess risks in the workplace to protect staff, students and others that can be affected, from the work undertaken by the University. Template and Guidance The Health, Safety and Compliance Team have created the following risk assessment template and guidance to help with the risk assessment process: Risk Assessment Template Risk Assessment Guidance Method Statement Form New and Expectant Mothers Risk Assessment New and expectant mothers are defined as those who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have given birth in the previous 6 months. Under regulation 16 of the Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations, a specific risk assessment may be required to ensure the safety of new and expectant mothers whilst at work. Consider the following: Review existing risk assessments and controls Consult with the individual to identify specific conditions and circumstances If further controls are required, a risk assessment will need to be created Young Persons Risk Assessment Young persons are defined as those under the age of 18. Under regulation 19 of the Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations, a specific risk assessment may be required to ensure

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Safe Use of Compressed Gas – Theory and Practical Target Audience: Technical, Academic and Research staff who use Compressed Gases. Aims To give members of staff a greater understanding of the hazards associated with compressed gases and how to comply with the Dangerous Substances and Explosive Atmospheres Regulations (DSEAR) 2002 and the Control of Substances Hazardous to Health 2002 (COSHH). The practical session will give candidates the opportunity to install gas handling equipment, such as 2 stage pressure reduction heads and flame arresters. This training will give participants an understanding of: - A legislative overview - The properties of gases - Types of cylinders and how to identify them - Hazards associated with compressed gases - Principles of combustion, including oxygen enrichment - Control measures - Requirements of relevant legislation The practical session will include manual handling of gas cylinders, connection and testing of gas handling equipment, maintenance and use of personal protective equipment. Learning Outcomes As a result of attending, participants will be able to: - Know the properties and classification of compressed gas - Identify the different types of compressed gas and cylinders - Identify the health hazards associated with compressed gases - Identify potential fuel and ignition sources - Select suitable control measures - Outline the requirements of legislation, including DSEAR and COSHH in relation to compressed gases - Safely install and test gas handling equipment Explore Training Biological Safety Biosafety Awareness COSHH - Chemical Safety and COSHH Dealing with Chemical Spills and Emergency Preparedness Drone Training DSE Online training Fire Awareness Fi

title: Health, Safety and Compliance
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Health, Safety and Compliance > Training Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Target audience: All University staff in particular members of staff who routinely work at height. The Health and Safety Executive (HSE) definition of working at height: "...a place is 'at height' if a person could be injured falling from it..." Statistics show that two thirds of all major injuries are caused by low falls (below 2m), therefore this course is aimed at people that also routinely work at low heights, such as using footstools and step ladders. This course is aimed at enabling those responsible for such work to understand what is required when carrying out such an assessment, i.e. legislative requirements and best practice. Aims This training will give participants an understanding of: - working at heights and height awareness including the Legal Duties imposed by the Work at Height Regulations 2005. - the Risk Assessment process required by law for all work at height. - at the safe systems of work for working at height using the BS 8437 'Hierarchy of Protective Measures', include avoidance, safe places of work and appropriate work equipment. - the most common items of work equipment used within the University campus... their selection, classification and inspection, including ladders, step ladders and footstools. Learning outcomes As a result of attending, participants will: - understand their responsibilities and duties under the Work at Height Regulations 2005. - be competent to work safely at height with strong hazard awareness and a sound theoretical knowledge and practical experience of assessing ladders, step ladders and footstools. - have the ability to assess the risks and requirements needed for safely working at height in their scope of work. - be able to select their job sp

title: Estates and Campus Services
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Estates and Campus Services > Soft Services (Campus Environment) Estates and Campus Services Information To ensure that your internal and external post is dealt with quickly and efficiently, please make sure that the following points are observed: Indicate class/service on the top left hand corner of the envelope. Unless otherwise indicated mail will always be sent by the cheapest option. Be sure to imprint your departmental stamp on the reverse of the envelope. This will assist us should correspondence be returned to the University. To save time in the Post Room and to prevent any unnecessary work, please use an elastic band to separate 1st and 2nd class letters, ensuring that all of the envelopes are facing in the same direction. The earlier you get your external mail to the Post Room, the sooner it will be dealt with. Large postings delivered at the end of the day may not meet with the Royal Mail collection at 4.30 pm. Please inform the Post Room of any bulk posting (internal or external mail) at the earliest possible opportunity. Always use a name, department and building on internal mail. Failure to do so can result in delay. Bulk posting for internal mail should be sorted by department not building. Deliveries to Halls of Residence are made once a day. Please ensure that any correspondence for the Halls are placed in the internal mail and sorted into separate addresses. Do not send cash via the mail. Departments should make their own arrangements for all other courier services, however, please feel free to enquire about Parcelforce prices for comparative costs. PLEASE NOTE: In exceptional circumstances the Post room Supervisor will open 'returned'

body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Estates and Campus Services > Soft Services (Campus Environment) Estates and Campus Services Parcelforce We now have three methods of despatch for International Items. 1. GLOBAL EXPRESS (Replaces International Datapost) A premiun rate service with items being delivered the next working day by 10.30 to the U.S, 12 noon to Canada and from two working days to the rest of the world. Items for this service MUST be in the Post Room by 14.30 each day and MUST include recipients telephone number and details of contents. N.B This service also attracts a premium rate. If your department does not currently have a 13.00 pick-up, please contact the Post Room for an additional collection. 2. GLOBAL PRIORITY (New Service) From two working days to Europe and the U.S, and three working days to most other destinations. 3. EUROPRIORITY BUSINESS (Replaces Euro 48) A two day service to most European Countries. Please contact the Post room for prices and/or any restrictions on the above services All the above items must be placed in your UK Mailbags and NOT in the white Royal Mail polylopes, which are for STANDARD airmail items only. In addition, please include a 'Notification of Despatch Form' for each parcel. Please contact the Post Room to ensure your items are collected ready for same day despatch. title: Degree apprenticeships | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Learn and Earn Earn a salary, get a degree, and pay no tuition fees Would you like to earn a salary while you get a Bachelor's or Master's degree, without paying tuition fees? Degree apprenticeships allow you to work as an employee while you study part time. They combine academic study from a traditional university degree with practical on-the-job experience and employment skills you need for a successful career. Browse our degree apprenticeships Learn skills in high demand Degree apprenticeships are developed between the Government, employers and universities, so you’ll learn skills that will be in high demand in the future. They're suitable for anyone over 18, whether you've just finished school or college, or you're already well into your career. The Government and your employer pay your tuition fees, so you won’t need to take out a tuition fee loan. Degree apprenticeships take 1–5 years to complete, depending on the course you take. You'll typically go to university 1 day a week for around 30 weeks every year. On the weeks you don't go to university, you'll spend a minimum of 6 hours per week studying outside of work. The Government's Office for Students website provides further guidance on degree apprentices for students, potential apprentices and employers. Types of employer you could work with We provide degree apprenticeships with various types of employer, from large multinational organisations to small local companies Previous employers include the Royal Navy, Portsmouth City Council, Hampshire County Council, Blanchard Wells, Estee Lauder, Mace, Osborne, Apollo Fire, Neilcott Constructions, Pfizer and Hampshire Chamber of Commerce. We regularly discuss new degree apprenticeships with employers. If you're interested in a role or subject not listed, get in touch with us and we can let you know if ot

title: Accessibility
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Accessibility The University of Portsmouth is committed to increasing the accessibility of information to all users, including visitors with visual or motor impairments. This statement outlines our activities and achievements so far in increasing accessibility. Should you encounter any problems in accessing these pages, or you would like to suggest improvements, please contact interact@port.ac.uk. Standards compliance Our aim is for all pages within the domain www.port.ac.uk to comply with priority 1 and 2 guidelines of the World Wide Web Consortium’s (W3C) Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI). We are striving towards validating all pages as XHTML 1.0 Transitional, whilst using structured semantic markup, with CSS 2.1 for page design. The use of tables for non-tabular information is being phased out, however some may remain in the transitional period of changeover from immediately after site launch to early 2008. Access keys Nearly all modern browsers support jumping to key links by typing keys defined on the web site. On Windows, this is done by pressing ALT + an access key, on Macintosh, press Control + an access key. All pages on this site define the following access keys: Access key 1 - Home page. Access key 2 - Skip to content. Access key 4 - Put cursor in the search box. Access key 0 - Help/Accessibility details (this page). Text resizing The text on this website is built to resize in order to be as flexible as possible to users with different needs. Text can be increased or decreased from your browser tool bar, or on Windows, if you have a mouse with a scroll wheel, you can hold down ALT and scroll the wheel backwards or forwards to alter the text size. Exceptions Within these pages there are links to external sites that are not part of the www.port.ac.uk domain. The University of Portsmouth cannot guarantee the level of complian

title: Why choose Portsmouth? | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Discover all Portsmouth has to offer We're a top 10 most affordable UK city for students Portsmouth is a great place to live and study. Here’s the proof. More for your money Save on rent – rent in Portsmouth is 54% lower than London and 25% lower than Brighton. (Numbeo, 2025) Save on living costs – NatWest ranks Portsmouth as one of the top 10 most affordable student cities. (NatWest Student Living Index 2024) Save on transport – you can walk to any of our uni buildings in under 15 minutes. Even better, there’s a free uni bus to take you to all the popular student spots. See our student accommodation Module choices Our wide range of module options means you can choose what interests you the most You asked, we listened - module choice rated the highest factor in choosing Portsmouth Module choices are available on the majority of our undergraduate and postgraduate taught courses Find your course Unique placements Portsmouth is the only UK university offering Connected Degrees We offer you the flexibility to choose when to take your placement: either before your final year or after you’ve completed your degree 3 years learning + 1 year earning, to suit you Explore Connected Degrees Wellbeing support If you need it, our Student Wellbeing team is on hand to provide personal and emotional support, mental health support and counselling Our Residence Life team are always on hand to manage any welfare and concerns about living in halls of residence "The services that the uni offer are so valuable and I would recommend utilising them if you ever need to - everyone is really friendly, helpful and impartial and have a vested interest in your wellbeing." (Roxanne Smith, MSc Health Psychology student) Discover our health and wellbeing support Outstanding teaching We're rated Gold in the latest Teaching Excellence Framewor

title: How we can help your business | University of Portsmouth
body: Skip to main content <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WHX6C84" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Take your organisation forward Big or small, new to the market or well-established – whatever your business, we can help it go further Whether you’re a start-up or a multinational, taking your business to the next level is hard work. But whatever the challenges or opportunities that lie ahead for your business, we can help. Find out more about all the ways we can help your business Innovation Connect: office space and business community If you're self-employed or running a small to medium enterprise and need space to grow, Innovation Connect offers desk and office space options in Portsmouth, as well as expert advice and support. Find out more Business services Is your business ready to take a step forward? We've got a long track record helping start-ups and businesses in different markets to grow and develop. Find out more Use our expertise We're home to experts in every field, who can help find concrete solutions to the challenges facing your business. Find out more Train your workforce From degree apprenticeships, to work-based Learning at Work programmes and bespoke training, we can help develop your employees’ skills. Find out more Work with our students and graduates New ideas, endless enthusiasm and expert knowledge – all qualities that our students and graduates can bring to businesses of any size. Find out more Labs and testing facilities From collaborative research work to product development and testing, our facilities are open for business. title: Recruitment, Marketing and Communications
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Recruitment, Marketing and Communications > Internal communications Recruitment, Marketing and Communications This document supports the Staff Email policy in providing information on what to include within an email signature and also how to add the signature and image. How to create an email signature Open your Gmail in a new browser window and access your account settings by clicking on the gear image in the top righthand corner. Select 'See all settings'. Scroll down to the 'Signature' section and enter your new signature text in the box. You can format your text using the buttons directly below the text box. To add the image, click the 'Insert Image' icon below the signature text box. Insert the image file for the version of the signature you wish to use. If you wish to use the most up-to-date University footer, you can do so by selecting the 'Web Address (URL)' insert option and entering the following URL: https://storage.googleapis.com/edm-email-content/email-signature1.png and clicking 'Select'. Once the image is pasted into your signature, click the image and select 'original size'. This will ensure that when the image is updated in the future the image will not stretch. Click 'Save Changes' at the bottom of the ‘general settings’ page. title: Music
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Support Departments > Music Music We offer lots of ways to get involved in music-making at the University of Portsmouth. Our aim is to provide accessible and enjoyable learning opportunities for students, staff and members of the local community. We bring diverse groups of people together to participate in music in a number of different settings. We also help individuals to realise their musical potential. We embrace all levels from beginners to advanced performers and provide a supportive, friendly and socially vibrant environment to engage with music. We offer specialist facilities, a range of different ensembles, numerous performing and listening opportunities, links with the curriculum and specialist staff to support musical learning. We also offer a range of musical activities for students who are interested in music, regardless of which degree they are studying for. Groups on offer include Student Chorus, Choir, Orchestra, Concert Band and Big Band. In addition, some instruments are available for hire, and private tuition can be arranged for most instruments, as well as voice and music theory. We can also organise access to individual practice rooms. For more information visit our What We Offer page. To book tickets for all of our concerts please visit the University's online store. If you are interested in musical activities, please register online. title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
body: <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> <iframe src="//www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-P6N6C8K" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Go back to: About Us > Departments > Departments > Equity, Diversity & Inclusion > Reports Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Staff Equality Monitoring Why is your data important? The University needs to understand how employees from all different backgrounds are being treated at work, and one way we do this is by gathering and analysing equality monitoring data (information such as age, disability, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, and belief). Knowing the composition of our workforce helps the University invest in the required support and facilities and address issues of under-representation, equity of treatment and opportunity. How is your data used? Equality monitoring information has previously helped us to identify and make positive changes to improve the experience of some staff groups. Positive changes include developing better prayer facilities, staff network development, transgender and disability awareness training and positive action measures in recruitment and staff development. Without equality monitoring information, we would not know whether our policies are working or whether the benefits we promote across campus are inclusive. A "prefer not to say" response is still helpful and provides much more insight than no response. There is still, however, a significant proportion of the staff equality monitoring information that is either missing or incomplete. If you are a staff member at UoP, we would appreciate a few minutes to update or complete your equality monitoring profile through the HR Self-Service System. Who can see your data? Access to the information you provide is restricted within HR to only those members of staff who need it for their work, to ensure confidentiality.
